Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider) (#1)

* add Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulation (3D)

New simulation mode: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion on a 3D volume,
rendered via fullscreen raymarch of an isosurface on the v-field.
Introduces the first texture-backed sim state (rg16float 3D ping-pong)
and the first raymarched renderer in the codebase — plumbing that
follow-up sims (Physarum, wave equation) can reuse.

Presets: Spots, Mazes, Worms, Mitosis, Coral.

* Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider)

Renders a floating 3D panel in immersive-vr mode so users can switch
simulation modes and tweak one primary parameter without leaving the
headset.

- New procedural SDF shader (src/shaders/xr.ui.wgsl) draws a rounded
  background, two chevron buttons, and a slider track/fill/knob in one
  quad — no textures, no glyph atlas.
- Per-mode slider bindings in main.ts map to an existing state field
  (e.g. fluid.forceStrength, physics.G).
- Ray-plane hit-testing routes pinch input between UI and simulation
  through a single-enforcer check in updateXRSimulationInteraction.
- selectstart defers resolution to the first XRFrame so hit-tests have
  a real pose; selectend triggers button actions or cleanly clears.
- UI renders into the HDR scene between sim.render and bloom so it
  picks up tonemap + glow.

* fix reaction sim black-screen and add per-frame error surfacing

Two bugs in the initial reaction-diffusion implementation:

1. rg16float is NOT in WebGPU's baseline storage-texture format list.
   Texture creation failed silently and the sim rendered garbage or
   nothing at all. Switched to rgba16float (always in the baseline),
   using only .rg channels in the shader. Fixed bytesPerRow (N*4 → N*8)
   and seed array stride (*2 → *4) accordingly.

2. Sim failures produced zero user feedback — black screen or freeze,
   with no way to know what went wrong. Now:

   - ensureSimulation() wraps factory creation in validation/internal/
     OOM error scopes. Any failure surfaces via showSimError() into the
     existing top-left red overlay.

   - frame() wraps sim.compute/render in a frame-level validation scope
     plus a try/catch. On any failure the broken sim is destroyed and
     dropped from the registry so the app stops hammering bad GPU state
     every frame. The user sees the error and can switch modes.

This addresses the "black screen / freeze / eject from XR" experience —
whatever goes wrong next will say so.

* reaction sim: fix diffusion scaling + raymarch aliasing

Two linked bugs produced the "frozen boxes with ribs" look and the
"changes when I move the camera" artifact:

Physics:
- The Laplacian divided neighbors by 6, but the canonical Gray-Scott
  atlas (Du=0.2097, Dv=0.105) assumes a UNIT-weight Laplacian. Effective
  diffusion was running at 1/6 strength, so most presets froze as the
  reaction term couldn't compete with the weak spread. Removed /6.
- dt was 1.0, which is right at the stability edge for Du=0.2097
  (limit ~1/(6·Du) ≈ 0.79). Lowered to 0.7 for headroom.

Rendering:
- stepCount=96 through a 3-unit volume meant step size ≈ texel size.
  Under camera orbit, the fixed-stride march aligned to different texel
  boundaries per frame, producing visible ribs that appeared to "change
  with the camera". Bumped to 256 and added a per-pixel hash-based
  start offset (stochastic sampling), which converts what would be
  coherent aliasing into smooth noise that bloom easily absorbs.
- Widened the soft-iso smoothstep band from ±0.04 to ±0.08 so sub-texel
  surfaces fade in rather than popping.

All five presets should now evolve recognizably within a few seconds:
Spots stabilizes into blobs, Mazes into maze patterns, Mitosis visibly
divides, Coral fills space, Worms produces worm-like channels.

* XR UI panel: text labels, ray reticle, fix arrow direction

- Added text labels (PREV / NEXT / current slider parameter) via a
  canvas-rasterized label atlas uploaded to a GPUTexture. Labels are
  redrawn on mode change with dynamic font sizing so long labels like
  "GRAVITY" auto-fit.
- Added a glowing reticle at the XR ray / panel intersection whenever
  a pinch is active, giving Vision Pro users visual feedback about
  where their gaze-then-hand-corrected ray is pointing (since natural
  input only exposes a ray during a pinch).
- Fixed chevron direction: prev button now points left (<) and next
  points right (>).
- Widened button boxes to 0.36 world units so each fits a chevron
  plus a 2:1 aspect label rect with breathing room.
- Non-uniform label atlas sub-rects (2:1 for buttons, 4:1 for slider
  label) so text isn't stretched when sampled at the panel surface.
- Single-enforcer mode-cycling refactor: introduced selectMode() used
  by both DOM tab clicks and XR prev/next so state.mode, DOM classes,
  and simulation registry move together.
- XR slider drags now call syncUIFromState() + saveState() on release
  so DOM sliders show the updated value when the user exits VR.

* reaction sim: remove visible cube, scatter-noise seed

Three user-visible issues addressed:

1. Invisible boundary cube: patterns were piling up against the hard
   clamped-coord texture edge and you could see the cube outline.
   Added Dirichlet boundary in the compute shader — a smooth band at
   r > 0.80 of half-size pulls every cell toward (u=1, v=0), creating
   a "reservoir" zone that absorbs patterns before they reach the wall.
   Patterns now die in the interior; they literally cannot escape.

2. Visible cube edges in render: added a spherical alpha falloff
   (1 - smoothstep(0.78, 0.95, radial distance)) so the render has no
   cube edges at all — only a soft spherical fade to invisible.

3. "Keeps growing the same blobs again" on reset: the old seed was 12
   big spherical blobs in a fixed central region, so every reset
   produced the same general silhouette regardless of preset. Replaced
   with a scattered noise seed — 80 tiny random points (1-2 cell
   radius) in the center 40% of the volume. Each reset now looks
   genuinely different and early-time patterns are fine-grained
   instead of obviously spherical.

The reset plumbing itself was already correct: applyPreset calls
resetCurrentSim which destroys + recreates the sim, so the factory
re-runs Math.random for every preset click.

* fix reaction time knob, bg race, nbody classic reserved word

1. Reaction time slider: timeScale now drives substep count, not dt.
   Previously multiplying into dt blew past the explicit-Euler stability
   bound (~0.79 for Du=0.2097), producing NaN and a disappearing sim.

Fix XR stereo: use per-view camera buffer offsets instead of overwriting

Root cause: sim.render() called device.queue.writeBuffer(cameraBuffer, 0, ...)
for each eye. writeBuffer is a queue operation that executes before any submitted
command buffer — so the right eye's write overwrote the left eye's data, and
both render passes used the right eye's view matrix.

Fix (proper approach, matching the WebXR barebones sample):
- Expand all camera buffers to CAMERA_STRIDE * 2 (2 × 256 bytes)
- Create per-view bind groups for all simulations and the grid renderer,
  each binding the camera buffer at its eye's 256-byte-aligned offset
- render() and renderGrid() accept viewIndex (default 0) and write to
  viewIndex * CAMERA_STRIDE, so left and right eye data coexist in the
  buffer without conflict
- Single encoder + single submit preserved — no extra overhead

forward convention). Simulation geometry at [-2,2]^3 then appears 5m ahead.

For local-floor, also apply y: +1.6 to raise the reference space to eye level
so the simulation center is at roughly eye height rather than at the user's feet.

feat: time-reversible N-body — Phase 1 (DKD leapfrog + dark matter)

fix(physics): unify attractor time base + clarify dark-matter GM semantics

Two related fixes surfaced by PR review:

1. Attractor strength time base. Forward compute was computing
   `nowSec = simStep * baseDt` for attractorStrength(), while chargeStart
   and releaseTime are stamped with `nowSeconds()` (wall clock). At low
   simStep values the two diverged enough to push `(now - chargeStart)`
   negative, which `Math.min(1, x)` happily forwarded into `t*t*ceiling`
   and produced huge spurious strengths on the first frames after a
   click. Switched the forward path to wall clock so every site that
   touches attractor lifecycle — chargeStart, releaseTime, prune, strength
   at composite, strength at compute — shares one clock. Reverse still
   replays journaled strengths verbatim, so determinism is unchanged.

2. Dark-matter force comments. Shader said `F = -G*M_halo * r / ...` as
   if G were a separate factor; the code (intentionally) omits params.G
   because params.G is normalized for the pairwise N-body sum and would
   crush the single-source halo/disk forces by ~1000x. haloMass and
   diskMass are GM-equivalent parameters. Updated the shader comments
   and darkMatterVcirc2() comment to document this so future readers do
   not "fix" the missing G and silently detune the galaxy.

refactor(physics): move simStep decrement before param packing

Previously, reverse-mode (timeDirection < 0) decremented simStep inside
the else-branch of attractor handling — AFTER tidal angle, sim clock, and
journal index were already computed from the pre-decrement simStep. That
meant the reverse step at simStep=N used the params from step N, then
decremented to N-1 after reading the journal at index N.

The consequence: forward and reverse at matching simStep values used
different params. On forward, step N uses simStep=N then increments to
N+1. On reverse, we want step N+1 → N to use the exact same params as
the original forward step that produced that state. Deferring the
decrement meant we were using the wrong clock and journal index.

Fix: decrement simStep at the top of compute() for reverse mode, so that
every downstream consumer (tidal angle at f32[7], attractor journal
lookup, `nowSec`) refers to the simulation step we're integrating back
toward — matching the original forward-step params exactly.

This is required for the time-reversal invariant: reverse(forward(s)) = s.

fix(xr): address PR review feedback

Remove unrelated simStep refactor; fix validation-scope double-pop; guard
depth override for non-array fallback; add follow-up polish.

1. Revert simStep refactor in createPhysicsSimulation
   Unrelated N-body time-reversal reordering (moved `simStep--` from the
   else-branch to before param packing) was accidentally included in the
   original depth commit. It's a semantic change to reverse-time physics
   that warrants its own review context. Reverted here; will ship in a
   separate focused PR.

2. Fix double-pop of validation error scope in xrFrame
   The `!pose` and `!sim` early-return paths were calling popErrorScope
   before returning, and then the finally block popped a second time.
   Since WebGPU error scopes are stacked, the second pop either returned
   a rejected promise (empty stack) or captured errors from a parent
   scope that didn't belong to us. Now only the finally pops.

3. Drop non-array + depth projection-layer configs
   When a non-texture-array layer is combined with a depth attachment,
   the XR-provided depth texture is full-width (2·eyeW) while our HDR
   scene is per-eye (eyeW, eyeH). Mixing them in one render pass fails
   validation. Non-array layers are now only used in depth-less fallback
   configs; xrDepthOverride additionally guards with a runtime
   textureArrayLength > 1 check so any future layer variant is safe.

4. Note: depth32float fallback intentionally omitted
   All sim pipelines compile against depth24plus. A depth32float layer
   would hand us a texture no pipeline can bind. Documented the
   reasoning inline so future readers don't reintroduce it without
   also dual-compiling pipelines.

5. Reset xrFrameCount on session.end
   Lets diagnostic logging re-emit first-frame info on session re-entry,
   which is critical for diagnosing intermittent XR failures where
   session 2 behaves differently from session 1.

6. Cap __errorLog at 200 entries (ring buffer)
   A device-lost loop or repeat-fire shader warning can produce hundreds
   of entries per second; unbounded growth would retain old stacks
   indefinitely.

feat(logging): comprehensive WebXR and WebGPU error diagnostics

Root issue: XR sessions were failing silently on Vision Pro — the permission
prompt appeared but the session never entered immersive mode, and nothing
was logged anywhere. WebGPU validation errors surface asynchronously via
onuncapturederror with no indication of which operation caused them, and
shader compile failures do not throw from createShaderModule at all.

Infrastructure (new Section 0):
- logError(kind, err, extra?): central error sink — console + DOM overlay +
  in-memory __errorLog array, all tagged with the current GPU phase
- currentGpuPhase: rolling label updated before every risky GPU/XR call so
  async errors can be attributed to the operation that caused them

  escapes our try/catch blocks

  Safari Web Inspector

Shader compilation:
- createShaderModuleChecked(label, code) wraps device.createShaderModule and
  schedules getCompilationInfo() — logs every error and warning with the
  offending source line quoted and a caret marker. All 23 shader-module
  call sites across boids / nbody / fluid / parametric / reaction / grid /
  xr.ui / post-fx migrated to the checked variant.

XR lifecycle:
- toggleXR now logs a phase transition before every step (requestSession,
  requestReferenceSpace, new XRGPUBinding, createProjectionLayer,
  updateRenderState, fixedFoveation, first frame requested)
- Projection layer fallback loop records every rejected config with its
  error message; if all configs fail the final error includes the full
  attempt log as JSON
- visibilitychange listener logs compositor state transitions
- session.end logs the final phase for post-mortem correlation

Per-frame scoping:
- xrFrame pushes a validation error scope around encoding; pops it in a
  finally block with error reporting so GPU validation failures get
  attributed to the frame number + sub-phase
- First 3 xrFrame invocations log entry, subImage metadata, and successful
  submit — enough to diagnose "session starts but nothing renders"
- Phase markers at every encode step (compute, per-eye getViewSubImage,
  createView color/depth, ensureHdrTargets, sim.render, UI pass, bloom,
  composite, submit) so uncapturederror reports pinpoint the failure

onuncapturederror + device.lost now route through logError so they inherit

fix(xr): submit scene depth to compositor for parallax-correct reprojection

Vision Pro's compositor reprojects each rendered frame to the actual head
pose at scanout (~11-16ms after render). Without a depth buffer, it can
only planar-warp — which shears the world during head motion and reads as
jitter/instability. Also sets fixedFoveation=0 to kill peripheral blur.

- Add xrDepthOverride module variable; getDepthAttachment uses it when set
- Projection layer configs now request depthStencilFormat='depth24plus'
  (depth-less configs kept as last-resort fallbacks)
- xrFrame binds subImage.depthStencilTexture per eye before sim.render
  and the UI overlay pass; overrides cleared after both eyes encode
- Set xrLayer.fixedFoveation = 0 for full peripheral resolution
- Log chosen layer config + texture dimensions to aid diagnosis

docs: rewrite CLAUDE.md for conservative physics + time reversal architecture

Comprehensive update reflecting the Phase 1+2 changes:

- DKD leapfrog integrator with inline half-drift (replaces Euler)
- All-conservative force inventory (dark matter halo + MN disk, no dissipation)
- Simulation clock (monotonic step counter, not wall-clock)
- Attractor wand system (charge/decay lifecycle, per-pointer tracking)
- Time reversal (journal, R key, boundary conditions, timeline branching)

feat: time reversal — attractor journal + R key to rewind (Phase 2)

Hold R (desktop) or the rewind FAB (mobile) to run the simulation
backwards in time. Release to resume forward. The DKD leapfrog
integrator is exactly time-reversible under dt negation — the reversed
simulation retraces the forward trajectory with full fidelity.

ATTRACTOR JOURNAL (9.3 MB circular buffer):
- Stores the packed attractor uniform array (count + 32 × pos/strength)
  at each simulation step — 129 floats × 18000 steps = 5 minutes of history.
- Forward: compute attractor strengths from live mouse state, write to
  journal[simStep]. Reverse: read from journal[simStep], skip live
  computation. This ensures reversed forces match the original exactly.
- Timeline branching: resuming forward after a rewind overwrites the
  old journal from the current step onward.

TIME DIRECTION:
- setTimeDirection(1 | -1) on the physics sim, toggled by R key hold.
- dt passed to shader = baseDt × timeDirection. Negative dt → DKD runs
  the exact inverse of the forward step.
- Boundary: can't reverse past step 0 → auto-pause.
- Step counter shown in stats bar (Step: 1234 ◀/▶).

ATTRACTOR DECAY SLIDERS (previously dead, now wired):
- attractorDecayDuration() reads state.physics.attractorDecayRatio/Cap
  instead of the hardcoded ATTRACTOR_DECAY_MULTIPLIER = 2.0.
- Single enforcer: one function owns max(MIN_DECAY, min(cap, ratio × hold)).

CLEANUP:
- Removed coreOrbit from describeParam() (dead after Phase 1).
- Added dark matter param descriptions (haloMass, haloScale, etc.).
- Removed damping from getStats() type signature (no longer returned).
- Attractor creation blocked during reverse (journal owns forces).

feat: time-reversible N-body with DKD leapfrog + dark matter potentials

Phase 1 of the reversible-physics architecture: replace all dissipative
forces with conservative dark matter potentials and switch to a
time-reversible integrator.

INTEGRATOR: DKD leapfrog with inline half-drift
- Semi-implicit Euler → Drift-Kick-Drift leapfrog
- Forces computed at half-step positions: each source particle is
  half-drifted inline during the tile load (srcHalf = src.pos + src.vel * dt/2)
- Exactly time-reversible: negating dt hits the same midpoint → same forces
- One force evaluation per step (same GPU cost as before)

FORCES REMOVED (7 dissipative, all velocity-dependent):
- Global damping, attractor friction, attractor drag
- Home reentry spring + damping, core friction
- Disk recovery: vertical/radial damping, tangential nudge, flow align

FORCES ADDED (2 conservative, position-only):
- Plummer dark matter halo: F = -M r / (r² + a²)^(3/2)
  Spherical confinement replacing global damping + boundary + home reentry
- Miyamoto-Nagai disk: F = -M (R_vec + n·z·a/B) / D³
  Axisymmetric vertical confinement replacing disk recovery forces

SIMULATION CLOCK: performance.now() → monotonic simStep counter.
Tidal angle = simStep × dt × rate. Fully deterministic.

VIRIAL CONTROLLER: removed entirely. Dark matter provides structural
stability without feedback. Stats reduction kept for diagnostics only.

INITIALIZATION: circular velocity formula now includes enclosed dark
matter mass (halo + MN disk) so particles start in equilibrium with the
combined potential.

Body struct: `home` field (initial position, used by removed home spring)
repurposed as `_unused`. Body stays 48 bytes for layout compatibility.

UI: "Disk Recovery" slider section replaced with "Dark Matter" section
(Halo Mass, Halo Scale, Disk Mass, Disk Scale A/B). Damping and Core
Friction sliders removed.

feat: extend zoom-out range from 50 to 200

Raises the camera distance clamp from 50 to 200 (desktop wheel + mobile
pinch) and bumps the far plane from 160 to 500 so geometry stays visible
at the new maximum distance.

fix: attractor decay sliders + review fixes (missed in PR #3 merge) (#5)

* fix: cache bind groups, tighten attractor lifecycle, fix uint64 timestamps

Several small hardening fixes:

**Bind group caching.** ensureHdrTargets already pre-builds fadeBGs,
downsampleBGs, upsampleBGs, and compositeBGs on resize, but the fade,
bloom, and composite passes were still calling device.createBindGroup
every frame, defeating the cache. Switch each pass to read from the
cached array. Downsample uses the [sceneIdx, i+1] mapping (ping-pong
sources live at indices 0-1, mip chain at 2+).

**Attractor pruning.** pruneAttractors() lived inside the physics sim's
compute path, so switching away from physics mid-decay would leak dead
attractors into state.attractors until the user returned to physics.
Move the prune call into the main frame loop (and the XR frame loop),
so the array stays clean regardless of active mode.

**XR wand resume.** XR pinch wand was keying on xrInteractionHasSample
(local bool) to decide create-vs-move. That flag gets reset on transient
tracking/pose losses, so a brief occlusion mid-pinch would orphan the
charging attractor and spawn a fresh one. Switch to
state.pointerToAttractor.has(XR_ATTRACTOR_POINTER_ID) — the authoritative
truth for "is there a held attractor for this pointer" — so drops resume
the same attractor.

**Uint64 timestamps.** WebGPU timestamp queries are unsigned 64-bit
counters; BigInt64Array would yield negative durations if the high bit
is ever set. Swap to BigUint64Array.

**Reticle strength range comment.** The per-attractor strength field is
actually in [0, interactionStrength ceiling] (up to ~3.0 at max slider),
not [0, 1]. mix() with s > 1 extrapolates beyond the anchor values,
which is intentional (a high-ceiling attractor should look stronger) —
just making that explicit in the shader comment.

* feat: decay ratio + cap sliders for attractor lifecycle

Attractor decay duration is now user-tunable via two new N-body sliders:

- **Decay Ratio** (default 0.5, range 0.1..4.0) — decay duration is
  this × holdDuration. Previous hard-coded value was 2.0 (long tail);
  0.5 gives a snappier feel where a 1s hold decays over 0.5s.

- **Decay Cap (s)** (default 2.0, range 0.5..10.0) — upper bound on
  decay duration. Prevents pathologically long decays on long holds
  (a 30s hold × 0.5 ratio would be 15s without a cap).

Decay duration is now computed in one place (attractorDecayDuration)
with `max(MIN_DECAY, min(cap, ratio × holdDuration))` — single enforcer,
clamped at both ends. Added a MIN_DECAY floor of 50ms so instant-tap
releases still play a visible decay animation.

Performance: zero cost. Two slider values are read once per frame in the
per-attractor strength computation (sub-nanosecond per attractor).
No shader changes, no uniform changes.
Prevent text selection on canvas for mobile long-press (#4)
feat: placeable attractor wand for N-body interaction (#3)

perf: revert tile size to 64 (benchmarked 64/128/256 — no significant difference)

Tile size benchmark at 80K particles (headless Chrome, macOS):
  64: 37-42 fps
  128: 37-39 fps
  256: 40-43 fps
All within noise. Bottleneck is force evaluation count (80K × 8192 = 655M/frame),
not workgroup size. Using 64 for lowest shared memory pressure.

feat: add FPS tracking to diagnostic output

Each diagnostic snapshot now reports FPS from __simState().
Headless Chrome baseline: 28-54 FPS across presets (GPU-limited in software).
Stats pass costs ~10% (35→32 fps at 100K) — not the primary bottleneck.

fix: count-independent init velocity for spiral galaxy

Init circular velocity now uses fixed REF_SOURCES=1000 * avgMass as the
reference total mass, so vCirc doesn't scale with particle count.
Previously, enclosedMass scaled with MASSIVE_BODY_COUNT while G_eff
divided by sqrt(MASSIVE_BODY_COUNT), giving sqrt(N) speed dependence.

Results: virial at t=0 now 0.74 (10K) and 1.09 (100K) instead of
0.58 and 0.55. 100K reaches near-perfect equilibrium (virial=0.98)
with no controller adjustment needed (tidal stays at 0.005).

fix: calibrate PE estimate + bidirectional virial controller

Stats shader: use actual mass (not clamped), G_eff (not G_raw), avg mass 0.9.
Controller: bidirectional — increases tidal when sub-virial, increases damping when super-virial.
Results: virial 0.58→0.85, armContrast 0.33→1.32 over 46s, system stable.

feat: virial equilibrium controller + GPU stats reduction shader

New nbody.stats.wgsl: single-workgroup reduction computing KE, PE estimate,
rmsRadius, rmsHeight, angular momentum, total mass in one pass.

Bidirectional virial controller (runs once/second):
- virial > target → increase damping + decrease tidal (cool the system)
- virial < target → increase tidal + decrease damping (heat the system)

Dynamic damping and tidal strength auto-adjust to maintain equilibrium.
Exposed via window.__simStats() for diagnostics.

NOTE: PE estimate in stats shader needs calibration — virial ratios
currently read too high (7-47 instead of ~1), likely due to G normalization
being applied twice. Controller logic is correct but input is wrong.

feat: spiral galaxy — rotating tidal bar + global damping equilibrium

Based on Duke wave dark matter paper: persistent spiral structure requires
a rotating non-axisymmetric potential (our tidal quadrupole) balanced by
energy dissipation (their inelastic collisions → our global damping).

tidal=0.03, damping=0.98: energy injection ≈ dissipation
- rmsRadius stable at 1.1-1.4 over 30s (radiusRate: -0.008)
- armContrast sustained: 0.83→1.26→0.82 (persists, doesn't dissolve)
- Flat disk: rmsH < 0.01 after t=7
- Dense core maintained: radial bin[0] always dominant

feat: spiral galaxy — exponential disk + vertical damping, arms persist 30s+

Reverted to exponential disk for massive bodies (no dark matter halo).
Simple vertical damping (diskVertDamp=1.0, diskVertSpring=0.3) keeps
disk coherent without killing in-plane arm dynamics.

Diagnostics over 30s:
- rmsRadius stable: 1.3→2.1 (slow expansion, contained)
- rmsHeight: 0.35→0.002 (flattens without going paper-thin due to init thickness)
- armContrast grows: 0.22→0.55→0.82→1.01 (arms strengthen over time!)
- Radial profile: centrally concentrated throughout (bin[0] 760-980)
- tangentialFrac: 0.81→0.70 (mild heating, orbits stay mostly circular)

feat: spiral galaxy — pure gravity, no disk recovery, all bodies in disk

All massive bodies follow same exponential disk profile as tracers (no dark
matter halo). Zero disk recovery — pure gravity drives dynamics.
LAMBDA=5, DISK_SCALE=3.5 concentrates particles centrally.

Results (100K particles, 30s):
- Dense core maintained: radial bin[0] stays 800-1000 throughout
- Arms emerge naturally: armContrast 0.29→0.93 by t=13, persists >0.69
- Slow expansion: rmsR 1.2→2.8 over 30s (radiusRate 0.05)
- Vertical heating: rmsH 0.32→0.68 (no flattening forces)

feat: spiral galaxy rewrite — barnes-hut style emergent arms

Replaced seeded spiral arms with smooth exponential disk + enclosed-mass
circular velocities. Arms emerge from N-body dynamics (swing amplification
of N-body noise in differentially rotating disk). Based on maxw179/barnes-hut.

- Exponential radial profile: r = exp(-lambda * U) * scale
- Circular velocity from integrated enclosed mass fraction
- G=1.5, softening=0.15 (was 2.0 — eliminated ring attractor)
- No radDamp/tangGain/alignGain — pure gravity drives arm formation
- armContrast reaches 0.46 at t=9s (emergent, not seeded)

feat: spiral galaxy tuned via quantitative diagnostics

G=0.6, zero radDamp/tangGain/alignGain — gravity alone holds the system,
no circularization forces to erase arm density contrast.
Dark matter halo spread to sqrt(random)*5 for flat rotation curve.
Init speed matches diskTangSpeed (removed 0.3x factor).
Massive body radii spread wider (big 0.2-2.5, medium 0.5-4.0).
Tidal 0.012 sustains arms — armContrast grows from 0.2 to 0.57 by t=31.
System oscillates rmsR 1.9→4.5, contained within maxR<11, flat (rmsH<0.01).

Spiral preset now places all massive bodies as a stationary spherical dark matter

Visible tracers are placed along spiral arms and orbit in the dark matter potential.

feat: add GPU diagnostic readback for N-body simulation analysis

- diagnose() method on physics simulation samples 2048 particles via staging buffer
- Computes: COM, RMS height/radius/speed, radial/angular density profiles,
  tangential velocity fraction, arm contrast, disk normal

feat: unique preset initial states + fix workgroup barrier uniform control flow

Presets now seed distinct particle geometries:
- Spiral Galaxy: logarithmic spiral arms with exponential radial profile
- Cosmic Web: shell with grid attractor perturbations for filaments
- Star Cluster: 5 merging Plummer-sphere sub-clumps
- Maelstrom: 4 counter-rotating tilted rings

Fix workgroupBarrier uniform control flow: replace early return with
alive flag so all threads participate in barriers. Remove self-skip
branch from inner loop (softening handles it). Default count 80K.

feat: N-body simulation redesign — slower convergence, emergent structure, richer visuals

- Rebalanced force model: count-normalized gravity, reduced masses, wider spatial scale
- Rotating tidal quadrupole seeds spiral arms via differential rotation

perf: tiled N-body compute with shared memory, eliminate hot-loop waste

- Shared memory tiling: source bodies loaded cooperatively into
  var<workgroup> tile (128 bodies per tile as vec4f pos+mass).
  Reduces global memory reads ~64-100x per workgroup.
- inverseSqrt() replaces 1.0/sqrt() for native GPU rsqrt instruction.
- Eliminated redundant dot(diff,diff) in inner loop.
- Pre-allocate params staging buffer once at init instead of
  allocating ArrayBuffer + typed array views every frame.
- Workgroup size 64 -> 128 for better occupancy.

perf: remove per-frame GPU error scopes, clarify XR grab hit-test

- Remove pushErrorScope/popErrorScope from the render loop. These ran
  every frame, creating 60+ promise allocations/sec and microtask churn.
  device.onuncapturederror (already wired up) catches validation errors
  without per-frame overhead. Targeted scopes in ensureSimulation()
  remain for creation-time error attribution.

- Clarify XR UI panel comment: hit-test half-extents (e.g. GRAB_HALF_H
  = 0.035) are intentionally larger than the shader's rendered SDF

Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider) (#1)

* add Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulation (3D)

New simulation mode: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion on a 3D volume,
rendered via fullscreen raymarch of an isosurface on the v-field.
Introduces the first texture-backed sim state (rg16float 3D ping-pong)
and the first raymarched renderer in the codebase — plumbing that
follow-up sims (Physarum, wave equation) can reuse.

Presets: Spots, Mazes, Worms, Mitosis, Coral.

* Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider)

Renders a floating 3D panel in immersive-vr mode so users can switch
simulation modes and tweak one primary parameter without leaving the
headset.

- New procedural SDF shader (src/shaders/xr.ui.wgsl) draws a rounded
  background, two chevron buttons, and a slider track/fill/knob in one
  quad — no textures, no glyph atlas.
- Per-mode slider bindings in main.ts map to an existing state field
  (e.g. fluid.forceStrength, physics.G).
- Ray-plane hit-testing routes pinch input between UI and simulation
  through a single-enforcer check in updateXRSimulationInteraction.
- selectstart defers resolution to the first XRFrame so hit-tests have
  a real pose; selectend triggers button actions or cleanly clears.
- UI renders into the HDR scene between sim.render and bloom so it
  picks up tonemap + glow.

* fix reaction sim black-screen and add per-frame error surfacing

Two bugs in the initial reaction-diffusion implementation:

1. rg16float is NOT in WebGPU's baseline storage-texture format list.
   Texture creation failed silently and the sim rendered garbage or
   nothing at all. Switched to rgba16float (always in the baseline),
   using only .rg channels in the shader. Fixed bytesPerRow (N*4 → N*8)
   and seed array stride (*2 → *4) accordingly.

2. Sim failures produced zero user feedback — black screen or freeze,
   with no way to know what went wrong. Now:

   - ensureSimulation() wraps factory creation in validation/internal/
     OOM error scopes. Any failure surfaces via showSimError() into the
     existing top-left red overlay.

   - frame() wraps sim.compute/render in a frame-level validation scope
     plus a try/catch. On any failure the broken sim is destroyed and
     dropped from the registry so the app stops hammering bad GPU state
     every frame. The user sees the error and can switch modes.

This addresses the "black screen / freeze / eject from XR" experience —
whatever goes wrong next will say so.

* reaction sim: fix diffusion scaling + raymarch aliasing

Two linked bugs produced the "frozen boxes with ribs" look and the
"changes when I move the camera" artifact:

Physics:
- The Laplacian divided neighbors by 6, but the canonical Gray-Scott
  atlas (Du=0.2097, Dv=0.105) assumes a UNIT-weight Laplacian. Effective
  diffusion was running at 1/6 strength, so most presets froze as the
  reaction term couldn't compete with the weak spread. Removed /6.
- dt was 1.0, which is right at the stability edge for Du=0.2097
  (limit ~1/(6·Du) ≈ 0.79). Lowered to 0.7 for headroom.

Rendering:
- stepCount=96 through a 3-unit volume meant step size ≈ texel size.
  Under camera orbit, the fixed-stride march aligned to different texel
  boundaries per frame, producing visible ribs that appeared to "change
  with the camera". Bumped to 256 and added a per-pixel hash-based
  start offset (stochastic sampling), which converts what would be
  coherent aliasing into smooth noise that bloom easily absorbs.
- Widened the soft-iso smoothstep band from ±0.04 to ±0.08 so sub-texel
  surfaces fade in rather than popping.

All five presets should now evolve recognizably within a few seconds:
Spots stabilizes into blobs, Mazes into maze patterns, Mitosis visibly
divides, Coral fills space, Worms produces worm-like channels.

* XR UI panel: text labels, ray reticle, fix arrow direction

- Added text labels (PREV / NEXT / current slider parameter) via a
  canvas-rasterized label atlas uploaded to a GPUTexture. Labels are
  redrawn on mode change with dynamic font sizing so long labels like
  "GRAVITY" auto-fit.
- Added a glowing reticle at the XR ray / panel intersection whenever
  a pinch is active, giving Vision Pro users visual feedback about
  where their gaze-then-hand-corrected ray is pointing (since natural
  input only exposes a ray during a pinch).
- Fixed chevron direction: prev button now points left (<) and next
  points right (>).
- Widened button boxes to 0.36 world units so each fits a chevron
  plus a 2:1 aspect label rect with breathing room.
- Non-uniform label atlas sub-rects (2:1 for buttons, 4:1 for slider
  label) so text isn't stretched when sampled at the panel surface.
- Single-enforcer mode-cycling refactor: introduced selectMode() used
  by both DOM tab clicks and XR prev/next so state.mode, DOM classes,
  and simulation registry move together.
- XR slider drags now call syncUIFromState() + saveState() on release
  so DOM sliders show the updated value when the user exits VR.

* reaction sim: remove visible cube, scatter-noise seed

Three user-visible issues addressed:

1. Invisible boundary cube: patterns were piling up against the hard
   clamped-coord texture edge and you could see the cube outline.
   Added Dirichlet boundary in the compute shader — a smooth band at
   r > 0.80 of half-size pulls every cell toward (u=1, v=0), creating
   a "reservoir" zone that absorbs patterns before they reach the wall.
   Patterns now die in the interior; they literally cannot escape.

2. Visible cube edges in render: added a spherical alpha falloff
   (1 - smoothstep(0.78, 0.95, radial distance)) so the render has no
   cube edges at all — only a soft spherical fade to invisible.

3. "Keeps growing the same blobs again" on reset: the old seed was 12
   big spherical blobs in a fixed central region, so every reset
   produced the same general silhouette regardless of preset. Replaced
   with a scattered noise seed — 80 tiny random points (1-2 cell
   radius) in the center 40% of the volume. Each reset now looks
   genuinely different and early-time patterns are fine-grained
   instead of obviously spherical.

The reset plumbing itself was already correct: applyPreset calls
resetCurrentSim which destroys + recreates the sim, so the factory
re-runs Math.random for every preset click.

* fix reaction time knob, bg race, nbody classic reserved word

1. Reaction time slider: timeScale now drives substep count, not dt.
   Previously multiplying into dt blew past the explicit-Euler stability
   bound (~0.79 for Du=0.2097), producing NaN and a disappearing sim.

2. Reaction render ping-pong was off-by-one: renderBGs[pong] was reading
   the STALE texture. Swapped mapping so render sees the latest write.

3. Error scope handlers were deleting whatever sim currently lived at
   simulations[mode], which could be a fresh one the user just reset.
   Now captures the sim instance and only drops it if still the same.

4. nbody.classic.render.wgsl: renamed struct field 'active' -> 'enabled'.
   'active' is a WGSL reserved keyword, caused compile failure at 26:26.

* XR UI panel: add grab handle for repositioning

Adds a visionOS-style grab bar at the bottom of the panel that lets
users pinch-drag to reposition the panel anywhere in 3D space.

- New 'grab' element type in hit-testing and hover encoding
- Thin rounded pill at y=-0.40 below the slider, lights up with
  camera.accent on hover
- Drag tracks the world-space delta between initial and current
  ray/Z-plane intersection, offsetting XR_UI_PANEL_CENTER each frame
- Panel position persists across pinch releases within a session
- Grab state cleanly cleared on selectend alongside other UI state

reaction and physics_classic to be silently omitted. Rather than adding
to the manual list, loop over Object.keys(DEFAULTS) — the canonical mode
registry — so any future mode added to DEFAULTS is automatically persisted
with no further edits.

fix(fluid): use dye alpha for surface height

Render shader was computing height from length(d.rgb), which scales
with the dye color vector's magnitude. Single mode (~1.12), rainbow
(~1.73), and temperature dyes therefore produced visibly different
surface heights for the same injected density. Use d.a — the
mode-invariant splat amount written by fluid.forces.wgsl — so all
presets render at the same scale.

Fix XR stereo: use per-view camera buffer offsets instead of overwriting

Root cause: sim.render() called device.queue.writeBuffer(cameraBuffer, 0, ...)
for each eye. writeBuffer is a queue operation that executes before any submitted
command buffer — so the right eye's write overwrote the left eye's data, and
both render passes used the right eye's view matrix.

Fix (proper approach, matching the WebXR barebones sample):
- Expand all camera buffers to CAMERA_STRIDE * 2 (2 × 256 bytes)
- Create per-view bind groups for all simulations and the grid renderer,
  each binding the camera buffer at its eye's 256-byte-aligned offset
- render() and renderGrid() accept viewIndex (default 0) and write to
  viewIndex * CAMERA_STRIDE, so left and right eye data coexist in the
  buffer without conflict
- Single encoder + single submit preserved — no extra overhead
fix webxr render

Move XR viewer back 5m so simulation is in front, not surrounding

Apply getOffsetReferenceSpace() after acquiring the reference space to shift
the user's reported pose 5 units behind the world origin (z: -5 in WebGL
forward convention). Simulation geometry at [-2,2]^3 then appears 5m ahead.

For local-floor, also apply y: +1.6 to raise the reference space to eye level
so the simulation center is at roughly eye height rather than at the user's feet.

Use npm install instead of npm ci in deploy workflow

Vite 8 rolldown has platform-native bindings. Lockfile generated on macOS
arm64 doesn't include Linux x64 emnapi packages CI needs, so npm ci fails.
npm install resolves correctly per-platform without lockfile sync errors.

Single-file HTML playground for experimenting with WebGPU compute shaders.

shapes), each with interactive controls, presets, and real-time parameter

Merge f8e40e38313541c425844ed2ab759e54012daada into acf6fbcb2fd5464c6c9dfb3e26903f7e551ab810

fix(physics): unify attractor time base + clarify dark-matter GM semantics

Two related fixes surfaced by PR review:

1. Attractor strength time base. Forward compute was computing
   `nowSec = simStep * baseDt` for attractorStrength(), while chargeStart
   and releaseTime are stamped with `nowSeconds()` (wall clock). At low
   simStep values the two diverged enough to push `(now - chargeStart)`
   negative, which `Math.min(1, x)` happily forwarded into `t*t*ceiling`
   and produced huge spurious strengths on the first frames after a
   click. Switched the forward path to wall clock so every site that
   touches attractor lifecycle — chargeStart, releaseTime, prune, strength
   at composite, strength at compute — shares one clock. Reverse still
   replays journaled strengths verbatim, so determinism is unchanged.

2. Dark-matter force comments. Shader said `F = -G*M_halo * r / ...` as
   if G were a separate factor; the code (intentionally) omits params.G
   because params.G is normalized for the pairwise N-body sum and would
   crush the single-source halo/disk forces by ~1000x. haloMass and
   diskMass are GM-equivalent parameters. Updated the shader comments
   and darkMatterVcirc2() comment to document this so future readers do
   not "fix" the missing G and silently detune the galaxy.

refactor(physics): move simStep decrement before param packing

Previously, reverse-mode (timeDirection < 0) decremented simStep inside
the else-branch of attractor handling — AFTER tidal angle, sim clock, and
journal index were already computed from the pre-decrement simStep. That
meant the reverse step at simStep=N used the params from step N, then
decremented to N-1 after reading the journal at index N.

The consequence: forward and reverse at matching simStep values used
different params. On forward, step N uses simStep=N then increments to
N+1. On reverse, we want step N+1 → N to use the exact same params as
the original forward step that produced that state. Deferring the
decrement meant we were using the wrong clock and journal index.

Fix: decrement simStep at the top of compute() for reverse mode, so that
every downstream consumer (tidal angle at f32[7], attractor journal
lookup, `nowSec`) refers to the simulation step we're integrating back
toward — matching the original forward-step params exactly.

This is required for the time-reversal invariant: reverse(forward(s)) = s.

Merge pull request #8 from brandon-fryslie/xr-depth-reprojection

fix(xr): submit scene depth for parallax-correct reprojection

fix(xr): drop unknown casts, move override cleanup to finally

- Use subImage.depthStencilTexture directly (typed as GPUTexture | null
  in webxr-gpu.d.ts); the unknown cast weakened TS's null-handling
  contract without adding safety.

- Move xrCameraOverride and xrDepthOverride null-assignment into the
  finally block. Previously, a throw inside the try left the overrides
  set, retaining a compositor-owned depth view past the frame boundary
  (compositor reclaims those between frames — unsafe) and leaking XR
  matrices into the desktop frame loop if the user exits VR after an
  error. The finally now clears both regardless of outcome.

fix(xr): address PR review feedback

Remove unrelated simStep refactor; fix validation-scope double-pop; guard
depth override for non-array fallback; add follow-up polish.

1. Revert simStep refactor in createPhysicsSimulation
   Unrelated N-body time-reversal reordering (moved `simStep--` from the
   else-branch to before param packing) was accidentally included in the
   original depth commit. It's a semantic change to reverse-time physics
   that warrants its own review context. Reverted here; will ship in a
   separate focused PR.

2. Fix double-pop of validation error scope in xrFrame
   The `!pose` and `!sim` early-return paths were calling popErrorScope
   before returning, and then the finally block popped a second time.
   Since WebGPU error scopes are stacked, the second pop either returned
   a rejected promise (empty stack) or captured errors from a parent
   scope that didn't belong to us. Now only the finally pops.

3. Drop non-array + depth projection-layer configs
   When a non-texture-array layer is combined with a depth attachment,
   the XR-provided depth texture is full-width (2·eyeW) while our HDR
   scene is per-eye (eyeW, eyeH). Mixing them in one render pass fails
   validation. Non-array layers are now only used in depth-less fallback
   configs; xrDepthOverride additionally guards with a runtime
   textureArrayLength > 1 check so any future layer variant is safe.

4. Note: depth32float fallback intentionally omitted
   All sim pipelines compile against depth24plus. A depth32float layer
   would hand us a texture no pipeline can bind. Documented the
   reasoning inline so future readers don't reintroduce it without
   also dual-compiling pipelines.

5. Reset xrFrameCount on session.end
   Lets diagnostic logging re-emit first-frame info on session re-entry,
   which is critical for diagnosing intermittent XR failures where
   session 2 behaves differently from session 1.

6. Cap __errorLog at 200 entries (ring buffer)
   A device-lost loop or repeat-fire shader warning can produce hundreds
   of entries per second; unbounded growth would retain old stacks
   indefinitely.

feat(logging): comprehensive WebXR and WebGPU error diagnostics

Root issue: XR sessions were failing silently on Vision Pro — the permission
prompt appeared but the session never entered immersive mode, and nothing
was logged anywhere. WebGPU validation errors surface asynchronously via
onuncapturederror with no indication of which operation caused them, and
shader compile failures do not throw from createShaderModule at all.

Infrastructure (new Section 0):
- logError(kind, err, extra?): central error sink — console + DOM overlay +
  in-memory __errorLog array, all tagged with the current GPU phase
- currentGpuPhase: rolling label updated before every risky GPU/XR call so
  async errors can be attributed to the operation that caused them
- window.error and window.unhandledrejection listeners catch anything that
  escapes our try/catch blocks
- window.__errorLog() / window.__gpuPhase() for remote inspection via
  Safari Web Inspector

Shader compilation:
- createShaderModuleChecked(label, code) wraps device.createShaderModule and
  schedules getCompilationInfo() — logs every error and warning with the
  offending source line quoted and a caret marker. All 23 shader-module
  call sites across boids / nbody / fluid / parametric / reaction / grid /
  xr.ui / post-fx migrated to the checked variant.

XR lifecycle:
- toggleXR now logs a phase transition before every step (requestSession,
  requestReferenceSpace, new XRGPUBinding, createProjectionLayer,
  updateRenderState, fixedFoveation, first frame requested)
- Projection layer fallback loop records every rejected config with its
  error message; if all configs fail the final error includes the full
  attempt log as JSON
- visibilitychange listener logs compositor state transitions
- session.end logs the final phase for post-mortem correlation

Per-frame scoping:
- xrFrame pushes a validation error scope around encoding; pops it in a
  finally block with error reporting so GPU validation failures get
  attributed to the frame number + sub-phase
- First 3 xrFrame invocations log entry, subImage metadata, and successful
  submit — enough to diagnose "session starts but nothing renders"
- Phase markers at every encode step (compute, per-eye getViewSubImage,
  createView color/depth, ensureHdrTargets, sim.render, UI pass, bloom,
  composite, submit) so uncapturederror reports pinpoint the failure

onuncapturederror + device.lost now route through logError so they inherit
phase attribution and DOM overlay rendering.

fix(xr): submit scene depth to compositor for parallax-correct reprojection

Vision Pro's compositor reprojects each rendered frame to the actual head
pose at scanout (~11-16ms after render). Without a depth buffer, it can
only planar-warp — which shears the world during head motion and reads as
jitter/instability. Also sets fixedFoveation=0 to kill peripheral blur.

- Add xrDepthOverride module variable; getDepthAttachment uses it when set
- Projection layer configs now request depthStencilFormat='depth24plus'
  (depth-less configs kept as last-resort fallbacks)
- xrFrame binds subImage.depthStencilTexture per eye before sim.render
  and the UI overlay pass; overrides cleared after both eyes encode
- Set xrLayer.fixedFoveation = 0 for full peripheral resolution
- Log chosen layer config + texture dimensions to aid diagnosis

Merge branch 'zoom-range' into reversible-physics

docs: rewrite CLAUDE.md for conservative physics + time reversal architecture

Comprehensive update reflecting the Phase 1+2 changes:

- DKD leapfrog integrator with inline half-drift (replaces Euler)
- All-conservative force inventory (dark matter halo + MN disk, no dissipation)
- Simulation clock (monotonic step counter, not wall-clock)
- Attractor wand system (charge/decay lifecycle, per-pointer tracking)
- Time reversal (journal, R key, boundary conditions, timeline branching)
- Post-processing bind group caching + per-attractor reticle rendering
- Updated params struct layout (608-byte uniform with attractor array)
- Body struct changes (home → _unused)
- Phase 3 gas grid forward reference
Add justfile
Add spiral galaxy doc

feat: time reversal — attractor journal + R key to rewind (Phase 2)

Hold R (desktop) or the rewind FAB (mobile) to run the simulation
backwards in time. Release to resume forward. The DKD leapfrog
integrator is exactly time-reversible under dt negation — the reversed
simulation retraces the forward trajectory with full fidelity.

ATTRACTOR JOURNAL (9.3 MB circular buffer):
- Stores the packed attractor uniform array (count + 32 × pos/strength)
  at each simulation step — 129 floats × 18000 steps = 5 minutes of history.
- Forward: compute attractor strengths from live mouse state, write to
  journal[simStep]. Reverse: read from journal[simStep], skip live
  computation. This ensures reversed forces match the original exactly.
- Timeline branching: resuming forward after a rewind overwrites the
  old journal from the current step onward.

TIME DIRECTION:
- setTimeDirection(1 | -1) on the physics sim, toggled by R key hold.
- dt passed to shader = baseDt × timeDirection. Negative dt → DKD runs
  the exact inverse of the forward step.
- Boundary: can't reverse past step 0 → auto-pause.
- Step counter shown in stats bar (Step: 1234 ◀/▶).

ATTRACTOR DECAY SLIDERS (previously dead, now wired):
- attractorDecayDuration() reads state.physics.attractorDecayRatio/Cap
  instead of the hardcoded ATTRACTOR_DECAY_MULTIPLIER = 2.0.
- Single enforcer: one function owns max(MIN_DECAY, min(cap, ratio × hold)).

CLEANUP:
- Removed coreOrbit from describeParam() (dead after Phase 1).
- Added dark matter param descriptions (haloMass, haloScale, etc.).
- Removed damping from getStats() type signature (no longer returned).
- Attractor creation blocked during reverse (journal owns forces).

feat: time-reversible N-body with DKD leapfrog + dark matter potentials

Phase 1 of the reversible-physics architecture: replace all dissipative
forces with conservative dark matter potentials and switch to a
time-reversible integrator.

INTEGRATOR: DKD leapfrog with inline half-drift
- Semi-implicit Euler → Drift-Kick-Drift leapfrog
- Forces computed at half-step positions: each source particle is
  half-drifted inline during the tile load (srcHalf = src.pos + src.vel * dt/2)
- Exactly time-reversible: negating dt hits the same midpoint → same forces
- One force evaluation per step (same GPU cost as before)

FORCES REMOVED (7 dissipative, all velocity-dependent):
- Global damping, attractor friction, attractor drag
- Home reentry spring + damping, core friction
- Disk recovery: vertical/radial damping, tangential nudge, flow align

FORCES ADDED (2 conservative, position-only):
- Plummer dark matter halo: F = -M r / (r² + a²)^(3/2)
  Spherical confinement replacing global damping + boundary + home reentry
- Miyamoto-Nagai disk: F = -M (R_vec + n·z·a/B) / D³
  Axisymmetric vertical confinement replacing disk recovery forces

SIMULATION CLOCK: performance.now() → monotonic simStep counter.
Tidal angle = simStep × dt × rate. Fully deterministic.

VIRIAL CONTROLLER: removed entirely. Dark matter provides structural
stability without feedback. Stats reduction kept for diagnostics only.

INITIALIZATION: circular velocity formula now includes enclosed dark
matter mass (halo + MN disk) so particles start in equilibrium with the
combined potential.

Body struct: `home` field (initial position, used by removed home spring)
repurposed as `_unused`. Body stays 48 bytes for layout compatibility.

UI: "Disk Recovery" slider section replaced with "Dark Matter" section
(Halo Mass, Halo Scale, Disk Mass, Disk Scale A/B). Damping and Core
Friction sliders removed.

feat: extend zoom-out range from 50 to 200

Raises the camera distance clamp from 50 to 200 (desktop wheel + mobile
pinch) and bumps the far plane from 160 to 500 so geometry stays visible
at the new maximum distance.

feat: extend zoom-out range from 50 to 200 (#6)

Raises the camera distance clamp from 50 to 200 (desktop wheel + mobile
pinch) and bumps the far plane from 160 to 500 so geometry stays visible
at the new maximum distance.

fix: attractor decay sliders + review fixes (missed in PR #3 merge) (#5)

* fix: cache bind groups, tighten attractor lifecycle, fix uint64 timestamps

Several small hardening fixes:

**Bind group caching.** ensureHdrTargets already pre-builds fadeBGs,
downsampleBGs, upsampleBGs, and compositeBGs on resize, but the fade,
bloom, and composite passes were still calling device.createBindGroup
every frame, defeating the cache. Switch each pass to read from the
cached array. Downsample uses the [sceneIdx, i+1] mapping (ping-pong
sources live at indices 0-1, mip chain at 2+).

**Attractor pruning.** pruneAttractors() lived inside the physics sim's
compute path, so switching away from physics mid-decay would leak dead
attractors into state.attractors until the user returned to physics.
Move the prune call into the main frame loop (and the XR frame loop),
so the array stays clean regardless of active mode.

**XR wand resume.** XR pinch wand was keying on xrInteractionHasSample
(local bool) to decide create-vs-move. That flag gets reset on transient
tracking/pose losses, so a brief occlusion mid-pinch would orphan the
charging attractor and spawn a fresh one. Switch to
state.pointerToAttractor.has(XR_ATTRACTOR_POINTER_ID) — the authoritative
truth for "is there a held attractor for this pointer" — so drops resume
the same attractor.

**Uint64 timestamps.** WebGPU timestamp queries are unsigned 64-bit
counters; BigInt64Array would yield negative durations if the high bit
is ever set. Swap to BigUint64Array.

**Reticle strength range comment.** The per-attractor strength field is
actually in [0, interactionStrength ceiling] (up to ~3.0 at max slider),
not [0, 1]. mix() with s > 1 extrapolates beyond the anchor values,
which is intentional (a high-ceiling attractor should look stronger) —
just making that explicit in the shader comment.

* feat: decay ratio + cap sliders for attractor lifecycle

Attractor decay duration is now user-tunable via two new N-body sliders:

- **Decay Ratio** (default 0.5, range 0.1..4.0) — decay duration is
  this × holdDuration. Previous hard-coded value was 2.0 (long tail);
  0.5 gives a snappier feel where a 1s hold decays over 0.5s.

- **Decay Cap (s)** (default 2.0, range 0.5..10.0) — upper bound on
  decay duration. Prevents pathologically long decays on long holds
  (a 30s hold × 0.5 ratio would be 15s without a cap).

Decay duration is now computed in one place (attractorDecayDuration)
with `max(MIN_DECAY, min(cap, ratio × holdDuration))` — single enforcer,
clamped at both ends. Added a MIN_DECAY floor of 50ms so instant-tap
releases still play a visible decay animation.

Performance: zero cost. Two slider values are read once per frame in the
per-attractor strength computation (sub-nanosecond per attractor).
No shader changes, no uniform changes.
Prevent text selection on canvas for mobile long-press (#4)
feat: placeable attractor wand for N-body interaction (#3)

perf: revert tile size to 64 (benchmarked 64/128/256 — no significant difference)

Tile size benchmark at 80K particles (headless Chrome, macOS):
  64: 37-42 fps
  128: 37-39 fps
  256: 40-43 fps
All within noise. Bottleneck is force evaluation count (80K × 8192 = 655M/frame),
not workgroup size. Using 64 for lowest shared memory pressure.

feat: add FPS tracking to diagnostic output

Each diagnostic snapshot now reports FPS from __simState().
Headless Chrome baseline: 28-54 FPS across presets (GPU-limited in software).
Stats pass costs ~10% (35→32 fps at 100K) — not the primary bottleneck.

fix: count-independent init velocity for spiral galaxy

Init circular velocity now uses fixed REF_SOURCES=1000 * avgMass as the
reference total mass, so vCirc doesn't scale with particle count.
Previously, enclosedMass scaled with MASSIVE_BODY_COUNT while G_eff
divided by sqrt(MASSIVE_BODY_COUNT), giving sqrt(N) speed dependence.

Results: virial at t=0 now 0.74 (10K) and 1.09 (100K) instead of
0.58 and 0.55. 100K reaches near-perfect equilibrium (virial=0.98)
with no controller adjustment needed (tidal stays at 0.005).

fix: calibrate PE estimate + bidirectional virial controller

Stats shader: use actual mass (not clamped), G_eff (not G_raw), avg mass 0.9.
Controller: bidirectional — increases tidal when sub-virial, increases damping when super-virial.
Results: virial 0.58→0.85, armContrast 0.33→1.32 over 46s, system stable.

feat: virial equilibrium controller + GPU stats reduction shader

New nbody.stats.wgsl: single-workgroup reduction computing KE, PE estimate,
rmsRadius, rmsHeight, angular momentum, total mass in one pass.

Bidirectional virial controller (runs once/second):
- virial > target → increase damping + decrease tidal (cool the system)
- virial < target → increase tidal + decrease damping (heat the system)

Dynamic damping and tidal strength auto-adjust to maintain equilibrium.
Exposed via window.__simStats() for diagnostics.

NOTE: PE estimate in stats shader needs calibration — virial ratios
currently read too high (7-47 instead of ~1), likely due to G normalization
being applied twice. Controller logic is correct but input is wrong.

feat: spiral galaxy — rotating tidal bar + global damping equilibrium

Based on Duke wave dark matter paper: persistent spiral structure requires
a rotating non-axisymmetric potential (our tidal quadrupole) balanced by
energy dissipation (their inelastic collisions → our global damping).

tidal=0.03, damping=0.98: energy injection ≈ dissipation
- rmsRadius stable at 1.1-1.4 over 30s (radiusRate: -0.008)
- armContrast sustained: 0.83→1.26→0.82 (persists, doesn't dissolve)
- Flat disk: rmsH < 0.01 after t=7
- Dense core maintained: radial bin[0] always dominant

feat: spiral galaxy — exponential disk + vertical damping, arms persist 30s+

Reverted to exponential disk for massive bodies (no dark matter halo).
Simple vertical damping (diskVertDamp=1.0, diskVertSpring=0.3) keeps
disk coherent without killing in-plane arm dynamics.

Diagnostics over 30s:
- rmsRadius stable: 1.3→2.1 (slow expansion, contained)
- rmsHeight: 0.35→0.002 (flattens without going paper-thin due to init thickness)

- Radial profile: centrally concentrated throughout (bin[0] 760-980)
- tangentialFrac: 0.81→0.70 (mild heating, orbits stay mostly circular)

feat: spiral galaxy — pure gravity, no disk recovery, all bodies in disk

All massive bodies follow same exponential disk profile as tracers (no dark
matter halo). Zero disk recovery — pure gravity drives dynamics.
LAMBDA=5, DISK_SCALE=3.5 concentrates particles centrally.

Results (100K particles, 30s):

- Arms emerge naturally: armContrast 0.29→0.93 by t=13, persists >0.69

Replaced seeded spiral arms with smooth exponential disk + enclosed-mass
circular velocities. Arms emerge from N-body dynamics (swing amplification
of N-body noise in differentially rotating disk). Based on maxw179/barnes-hut.

- Exponential radial profile: r = exp(-lambda * U) * scale
- Circular velocity from integrated enclosed mass fraction
- G=1.5, softening=0.15 (was 2.0 — eliminated ring attractor)

Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider) (#1)

* add Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulation (3D)

New simulation mode: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion on a 3D volume,
rendered via fullscreen raymarch of an isosurface on the v-field.
Introduces the first texture-backed sim state (rg16float 3D ping-pong)
and the first raymarched renderer in the codebase — plumbing that
follow-up sims (Physarum, wave equation) can reuse.

Presets: Spots, Mazes, Worms, Mitosis, Coral.

* Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider)

Renders a floating 3D panel in immersive-vr mode so users can switch
simulation modes and tweak one primary parameter without leaving the
headset.

- New procedural SDF shader (src/shaders/xr.ui.wgsl) draws a rounded
  background, two chevron buttons, and a slider track/fill/knob in one
  quad — no textures, no glyph atlas.
- Per-mode slider bindings in main.ts map to an existing state field
  (e.g. fluid.forceStrength, physics.G).
- Ray-plane hit-testing routes pinch input between UI and simulation
  through a single-enforcer check in updateXRSimulationInteraction.
- selectstart defers resolution to the first XRFrame so hit-tests have
  a real pose; selectend triggers button actions or cleanly clears.
- UI renders into the HDR scene between sim.render and bloom so it
  picks up tonemap + glow.

* fix reaction sim black-screen and add per-frame error surfacing

Two bugs in the initial reaction-diffusion implementation:

1. rg16float is NOT in WebGPU's baseline storage-texture format list.
   Texture creation failed silently and the sim rendered garbage or
   nothing at all. Switched to rgba16float (always in the baseline),
   using only .rg channels in the shader. Fixed bytesPerRow (N*4 → N*8)
   and seed array stride (*2 → *4) accordingly.

2. Sim failures produced zero user feedback — black screen or freeze,
   with no way to know what went wrong. Now:

   - ensureSimulation() wraps factory creation in validation/internal/
     OOM error scopes. Any failure surfaces via showSimError() into the
     existing top-left red overlay.

   - frame() wraps sim.compute/render in a frame-level validation scope
     plus a try/catch. On any failure the broken sim is destroyed and
     dropped from the registry so the app stops hammering bad GPU state
     every frame. The user sees the error and can switch modes.

This addresses the "black screen / freeze / eject from XR" experience —
whatever goes wrong next will say so.

* reaction sim: fix diffusion scaling + raymarch aliasing

Two linked bugs produced the "frozen boxes with ribs" look and the
"changes when I move the camera" artifact:

Physics:
- The Laplacian divided neighbors by 6, but the canonical Gray-Scott
  atlas (Du=0.2097, Dv=0.105) assumes a UNIT-weight Laplacian. Effective
  diffusion was running at 1/6 strength, so most presets froze as the
  reaction term couldn't compete with the weak spread. Removed /6.
- dt was 1.0, which is right at the stability edge for Du=0.2097
  (limit ~1/(6·Du) ≈ 0.79). Lowered to 0.7 for headroom.

Rendering:
- stepCount=96 through a 3-unit volume meant step size ≈ texel size.
  Under camera orbit, the fixed-stride march aligned to different texel
  boundaries per frame, producing visible ribs that appeared to "change
  with the camera". Bumped to 256 and added a per-pixel hash-based
  start offset (stochastic sampling), which converts what would be
  coherent aliasing into smooth noise that bloom easily absorbs.
- Widened the soft-iso smoothstep band from ±0.04 to ±0.08 so sub-texel
  surfaces fade in rather than popping.

All five presets should now evolve recognizably within a few seconds:
Spots stabilizes into blobs, Mazes into maze patterns, Mitosis visibly
divides, Coral fills space, Worms produces worm-like channels.

* XR UI panel: text labels, ray reticle, fix arrow direction

- Added text labels (PREV / NEXT / current slider parameter) via a
  canvas-rasterized label atlas uploaded to a GPUTexture. Labels are
  redrawn on mode change with dynamic font sizing so long labels like
  "GRAVITY" auto-fit.
- Added a glowing reticle at the XR ray / panel intersection whenever
  a pinch is active, giving Vision Pro users visual feedback about
  where their gaze-then-hand-corrected ray is pointing (since natural
  input only exposes a ray during a pinch).
- Fixed chevron direction: prev button now points left (<) and next
  points right (>).
- Widened button boxes to 0.36 world units so each fits a chevron
  plus a 2:1 aspect label rect with breathing room.
- Non-uniform label atlas sub-rects (2:1 for buttons, 4:1 for slider
  label) so text isn't stretched when sampled at the panel surface.
- Single-enforcer mode-cycling refactor: introduced selectMode() used
  by both DOM tab clicks and XR prev/next so state.mode, DOM classes,
  and simulation registry move together.
- XR slider drags now call syncUIFromState() + saveState() on release
  so DOM sliders show the updated value when the user exits VR.

* reaction sim: remove visible cube, scatter-noise seed

Three user-visible issues addressed:

1. Invisible boundary cube: patterns were piling up against the hard
   clamped-coord texture edge and you could see the cube outline.
   Added Dirichlet boundary in the compute shader — a smooth band at
   r > 0.80 of half-size pulls every cell toward (u=1, v=0), creating
   a "reservoir" zone that absorbs patterns before they reach the wall.
   Patterns now die in the interior; they literally cannot escape.

2. Visible cube edges in render: added a spherical alpha falloff
   (1 - smoothstep(0.78, 0.95, radial distance)) so the render has no
   cube edges at all — only a soft spherical fade to invisible.

3. "Keeps growing the same blobs again" on reset: the old seed was 12
   big spherical blobs in a fixed central region, so every reset
   produced the same general silhouette regardless of preset. Replaced
   with a scattered noise seed — 80 tiny random points (1-2 cell
   radius) in the center 40% of the volume. Each reset now looks
   genuinely different and early-time patterns are fine-grained
   instead of obviously spherical.

The reset plumbing itself was already correct: applyPreset calls
resetCurrentSim which destroys + recreates the sim, so the factory
re-runs Math.random for every preset click.

* fix reaction time knob, bg race, nbody classic reserved word

1. Reaction time slider: timeScale now drives substep count, not dt.
   Previously multiplying into dt blew past the explicit-Euler stability
   bound (~0.79 for Du=0.2097), producing NaN and a disappearing sim.

2. Reaction render ping-pong was off-by-one: renderBGs[pong] was reading
   the STALE texture. Swapped mapping so render sees the latest write.

3. Error scope handlers were deleting whatever sim currently lived at
   simulations[mode], which could be a fresh one the user just reset.
   Now captures the sim instance and only drops it if still the same.

4. nbody.classic.render.wgsl: renamed struct field 'active' -> 'enabled'.
   'active' is a WGSL reserved keyword, caused compile failure at 26:26.

* XR UI panel: add grab handle for repositioning

Adds a visionOS-style grab bar at the bottom of the panel that lets
users pinch-drag to reposition the panel anywhere in 3D space.

- New 'grab' element type in hit-testing and hover encoding
- Thin rounded pill at y=-0.40 below the slider, lights up with
  camera.accent on hover
- Drag tracks the world-space delta between initial and current
  ray/Z-plane intersection, offsetting XR_UI_PANEL_CENTER each frame
- Panel position persists across pinch releases within a session
- Grab state cleanly cleared on selectend alongside other UI state

fix(fluid): use dye alpha for surface height

Render shader was computing height from length(d.rgb), which scales
with the dye color vector's magnitude. Single mode (~1.12), rainbow
(~1.73), and temperature dyes therefore produced visibly different
surface heights for the same injected density. Use d.a — the
mode-invariant splat amount written by fluid.forces.wgsl — so all
presets render at the same scale.

Fix XR stereo: use per-view camera buffer offsets instead of overwriting

Root cause: sim.render() called device.queue.writeBuffer(cameraBuffer, 0, ...)
for each eye. writeBuffer is a queue operation that executes before any submitted
command buffer — so the right eye's write overwrote the left eye's data, and
both render passes used the right eye's view matrix.

Fix (proper approach, matching the WebXR barebones sample):
- Expand all camera buffers to CAMERA_STRIDE * 2 (2 × 256 bytes)
- Create per-view bind groups for all simulations and the grid renderer,
  each binding the camera buffer at its eye's 256-byte-aligned offset
- render() and renderGrid() accept viewIndex (default 0) and write to
  viewIndex * CAMERA_STRIDE, so left and right eye data coexist in the
  buffer without conflict
- Single encoder + single submit preserved — no extra overhead
fix webxr render

Move XR viewer back 5m so simulation is in front, not surrounding

Apply getOffsetReferenceSpace() after acquiring the reference space to shift
the user's reported pose 5 units behind the world origin (z: -5 in WebGL
forward convention). Simulation geometry at [-2,2]^3 then appears 5m ahead.

For local-floor, also apply y: +1.6 to raise the reference space to eye level
so the simulation center is at roughly eye height rather than at the user's feet.

WebGPU compute shader playground with WebXR stereo rendering

Single-file HTML playground for experimenting with WebGPU compute shaders.
Four simulation modes (boids, N-body physics, fluid dynamics, parametric
shapes), each with interactive controls, presets, and real-time parameter
modulation. Features include:

- 3D rendering with orbit camera, background reference grid with animated pulses
- WebXR stereo rendering for Apple Vision Pro (XRGPUBinding path)
- Color palette system with 10 curated themes from popular editor themes

  surface deformation for parametric shapes)

fix: attractor decay sliders + review fixes (missed in PR #3 merge) (#5)

* fix: cache bind groups, tighten attractor lifecycle, fix uint64 timestamps

Several small hardening fixes:

**Bind group caching.** ensureHdrTargets already pre-builds fadeBGs,
downsampleBGs, upsampleBGs, and compositeBGs on resize, but the fade,
bloom, and composite passes were still calling device.createBindGroup
every frame, defeating the cache. Switch each pass to read from the
cached array. Downsample uses the [sceneIdx, i+1] mapping (ping-pong
sources live at indices 0-1, mip chain at 2+).

**Attractor pruning.** pruneAttractors() lived inside the physics sim's
compute path, so switching away from physics mid-decay would leak dead
attractors into state.attractors until the user returned to physics.
Move the prune call into the main frame loop (and the XR frame loop),
so the array stays clean regardless of active mode.

**XR wand resume.** XR pinch wand was keying on xrInteractionHasSample
(local bool) to decide create-vs-move. That flag gets reset on transient
tracking/pose losses, so a brief occlusion mid-pinch would orphan the
charging attractor and spawn a fresh one. Switch to
state.pointerToAttractor.has(XR_ATTRACTOR_POINTER_ID) — the authoritative
truth for "is there a held attractor for this pointer" — so drops resume
the same attractor.

**Uint64 timestamps.** WebGPU timestamp queries are unsigned 64-bit
counters; BigInt64Array would yield negative durations if the high bit
is ever set. Swap to BigUint64Array.

**Reticle strength range comment.** The per-attractor strength field is
actually in [0, interactionStrength ceiling] (up to ~3.0 at max slider),
not [0, 1]. mix() with s > 1 extrapolates beyond the anchor values,
which is intentional (a high-ceiling attractor should look stronger) —
just making that explicit in the shader comment.

* feat: decay ratio + cap sliders for attractor lifecycle

Attractor decay duration is now user-tunable via two new N-body sliders:

- **Decay Ratio** (default 0.5, range 0.1..4.0) — decay duration is
  this × holdDuration. Previous hard-coded value was 2.0 (long tail);
  0.5 gives a snappier feel where a 1s hold decays over 0.5s.

- **Decay Cap (s)** (default 2.0, range 0.5..10.0) — upper bound on
  decay duration. Prevents pathologically long decays on long holds
  (a 30s hold × 0.5 ratio would be 15s without a cap).

Decay duration is now computed in one place (attractorDecayDuration)
with `max(MIN_DECAY, min(cap, ratio × holdDuration))` — single enforcer,
clamped at both ends. Added a MIN_DECAY floor of 50ms so instant-tap
releases still play a visible decay animation.

Performance: zero cost. Two slider values are read once per frame in the
per-attractor strength computation (sub-nanosecond per attractor).
No shader changes, no uniform changes.

Tile size benchmark at 80K particles (headless Chrome, macOS):

  128: 37-39 fps

All within noise. Bottleneck is force evaluation count (80K × 8192 = 655M/frame),
not workgroup size. Using 64 for lowest shared memory pressure.

Headless Chrome baseline: 28-54 FPS across presets (GPU-limited in software).

fix: count-independent init velocity for spiral galaxy

Init circular velocity now uses fixed REF_SOURCES=1000 * avgMass as the
reference total mass, so vCirc doesn't scale with particle count.
Previously, enclosedMass scaled with MASSIVE_BODY_COUNT while G_eff
divided by sqrt(MASSIVE_BODY_COUNT), giving sqrt(N) speed dependence.

0.58 and 0.55. 100K reaches near-perfect equilibrium (virial=0.98)
with no controller adjustment needed (tidal stays at 0.005).

fix: calibrate PE estimate + bidirectional virial controller

Stats shader: use actual mass (not clamped), G_eff (not G_raw), avg mass 0.9.
Controller: bidirectional — increases tidal when sub-virial, increases damping when super-virial.
Results: virial 0.58→0.85, armContrast 0.33→1.32 over 46s, system stable.

feat: virial equilibrium controller + GPU stats reduction shader

New nbody.stats.wgsl: single-workgroup reduction computing KE, PE estimate,
rmsRadius, rmsHeight, angular momentum, total mass in one pass.

Bidirectional virial controller (runs once/second):
- virial > target → increase damping + decrease tidal (cool the system)
- virial < target → increase tidal + decrease damping (heat the system)

Dynamic damping and tidal strength auto-adjust to maintain equilibrium.
Exposed via window.__simStats() for diagnostics.

NOTE: PE estimate in stats shader needs calibration — virial ratios
currently read too high (7-47 instead of ~1), likely due to G normalization
being applied twice. Controller logic is correct but input is wrong.

feat: spiral galaxy — rotating tidal bar + global damping equilibrium

Based on Duke wave dark matter paper: persistent spiral structure requires
a rotating non-axisymmetric potential (our tidal quadrupole) balanced by
energy dissipation (their inelastic collisions → our global damping).

tidal=0.03, damping=0.98: energy injection ≈ dissipation
- rmsRadius stable at 1.1-1.4 over 30s (radiusRate: -0.008)
- armContrast sustained: 0.83→1.26→0.82 (persists, doesn't dissolve)
- Flat disk: rmsH < 0.01 after t=7
- Dense core maintained: radial bin[0] always dominant

feat: spiral galaxy — exponential disk + vertical damping, arms persist 30s+

Reverted to exponential disk for massive bodies (no dark matter halo).
Simple vertical damping (diskVertDamp=1.0, diskVertSpring=0.3) keeps
disk coherent without killing in-plane arm dynamics.

Diagnostics over 30s:
- rmsRadius stable: 1.3→2.1 (slow expansion, contained)
- rmsHeight: 0.35→0.002 (flattens without going paper-thin due to init thickness)
- armContrast grows: 0.22→0.55→0.82→1.01 (arms strengthen over time!)
- Radial profile: centrally concentrated throughout (bin[0] 760-980)
- tangentialFrac: 0.81→0.70 (mild heating, orbits stay mostly circular)

feat: spiral galaxy — pure gravity, no disk recovery, all bodies in disk

All massive bodies follow same exponential disk profile as tracers (no dark
matter halo). Zero disk recovery — pure gravity drives dynamics.
LAMBDA=5, DISK_SCALE=3.5 concentrates particles centrally.

Results (100K particles, 30s):
- Dense core maintained: radial bin[0] stays 800-1000 throughout
- Arms emerge naturally: armContrast 0.29→0.93 by t=13, persists >0.69
- Slow expansion: rmsR 1.2→2.8 over 30s (radiusRate 0.05)
- Vertical heating: rmsH 0.32→0.68 (no flattening forces)

feat: spiral galaxy rewrite — barnes-hut style emergent arms

Replaced seeded spiral arms with smooth exponential disk + enclosed-mass
circular velocities. Arms emerge from N-body dynamics (swing amplification
of N-body noise in differentially rotating disk). Based on maxw179/barnes-hut.

- Exponential radial profile: r = exp(-lambda * U) * scale
- Circular velocity from integrated enclosed mass fraction
- G=1.5, softening=0.15 (was 2.0 — eliminated ring attractor)
- No radDamp/tangGain/alignGain — pure gravity drives arm formation
- armContrast reaches 0.46 at t=9s (emergent, not seeded)

feat: spiral galaxy tuned via quantitative diagnostics

G=0.6, zero radDamp/tangGain/alignGain — gravity alone holds the system,
no circularization forces to erase arm density contrast.
Dark matter halo spread to sqrt(random)*5 for flat rotation curve.
Init speed matches diskTangSpeed (removed 0.3x factor).
Massive body radii spread wider (big 0.2-2.5, medium 0.5-4.0).
Tidal 0.012 sustains arms — armContrast grows from 0.2 to 0.57 by t=31.
System oscillates rmsR 1.9→4.5, contained within maxR<11, flat (rmsH<0.01).

scripts/diagnose-preset.sh — self-contained: starts dev server + headless
Chrome, switches to a preset, captures diagnostic readbacks at multiple

feat: dark matter halo for spiral galaxy — invisible mass creates stable potential well

Spiral preset now places all massive bodies as a stationary spherical dark matter

Visible tracers are placed along spiral arms and orbit in the dark matter potential.

feat: spiral galaxy — zero radial damping and tangential gain for arm preservation

radDamp=0, tangGain=0, alignGain=0, coreOrbit=0: no circularization forces

feat: add GPU diagnostic readback for N-body simulation analysis

- diagnose() method on physics simulation samples 2048 particles via staging buffer

feat: unique preset initial states + fix workgroup barrier uniform control flow

Presets now seed distinct particle geometries:
- Spiral Galaxy: logarithmic spiral arms with exponential radial profile
- Cosmic Web: shell with grid attractor perturbations for filaments
- Star Cluster: 5 merging Plummer-sphere sub-clumps
- Maelstrom: 4 counter-rotating tilted rings

Fix workgroupBarrier uniform control flow: replace early return with
alive flag so all threads participate in barriers. Remove self-skip

feat: N-body simulation redesign — slower convergence, emergent structure, richer visuals

- Rebalanced force model: count-normalized gravity, reduced masses, wider spatial scale
