perf: tiled N-body compute with shared memory, eliminate hot-loop waste

- Shared memory tiling: source bodies loaded cooperatively into
  var<workgroup> tile (128 bodies per tile as vec4f pos+mass).
  Reduces global memory reads ~64-100x per workgroup.
- inverseSqrt() replaces 1.0/sqrt() for native GPU rsqrt instruction.
- Eliminated redundant dot(diff,diff) in inner loop.
- Pre-allocate params staging buffer once at init instead of
  allocating ArrayBuffer + typed array views every frame.
- Workgroup size 64 -> 128 for better occupancy.

perf: remove per-frame GPU error scopes, clarify XR grab hit-test

  every frame, creating 60+ promise allocations/sec and microtask churn.
  device.onuncapturederror (already wired up) catches validation errors
  without per-frame overhead. Targeted scopes in ensureSimulation()
  remain for creation-time error attribution.

- Clarify XR UI panel comment: hit-test half-extents (e.g. GRAB_HALF_H
  = 0.035) are intentionally larger than the shader's rendered SDF
  extents (0.02) for easier targeting. The comment previously said
  "mirrored" which implied they should match.

Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider) (#1)

* add Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulation (3D)

New simulation mode: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion on a 3D volume,
rendered via fullscreen raymarch of an isosurface on the v-field.
Introduces the first texture-backed sim state (rg16float 3D ping-pong)
and the first raymarched renderer in the codebase — plumbing that
follow-up sims (Physarum, wave equation) can reuse.

Presets: Spots, Mazes, Worms, Mitosis, Coral.

* Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider)

Renders a floating 3D panel in immersive-vr mode so users can switch
simulation modes and tweak one primary parameter without leaving the
headset.

- New procedural SDF shader (src/shaders/xr.ui.wgsl) draws a rounded
  background, two chevron buttons, and a slider track/fill/knob in one
  quad — no textures, no glyph atlas.
- Per-mode slider bindings in main.ts map to an existing state field
  (e.g. fluid.forceStrength, physics.G).
- Ray-plane hit-testing routes pinch input between UI and simulation
  through a single-enforcer check in updateXRSimulationInteraction.
- selectstart defers resolution to the first XRFrame so hit-tests have
  a real pose; selectend triggers button actions or cleanly clears.
- UI renders into the HDR scene between sim.render and bloom so it
  picks up tonemap + glow.

* fix reaction sim black-screen and add per-frame error surfacing

Two bugs in the initial reaction-diffusion implementation:

1. rg16float is NOT in WebGPU's baseline storage-texture format list.
   Texture creation failed silently and the sim rendered garbage or
   nothing at all. Switched to rgba16float (always in the baseline),
   using only .rg channels in the shader. Fixed bytesPerRow (N*4 → N*8)
   and seed array stride (*2 → *4) accordingly.

2. Sim failures produced zero user feedback — black screen or freeze,
   with no way to know what went wrong. Now:

   - ensureSimulation() wraps factory creation in validation/internal/
     OOM error scopes. Any failure surfaces via showSimError() into the
     existing top-left red overlay.

   - frame() wraps sim.compute/render in a frame-level validation scope
     plus a try/catch. On any failure the broken sim is destroyed and
     dropped from the registry so the app stops hammering bad GPU state
     every frame. The user sees the error and can switch modes.

This addresses the "black screen / freeze / eject from XR" experience —
whatever goes wrong next will say so.

* reaction sim: fix diffusion scaling + raymarch aliasing

Two linked bugs produced the "frozen boxes with ribs" look and the
"changes when I move the camera" artifact:

Physics:
- The Laplacian divided neighbors by 6, but the canonical Gray-Scott
  atlas (Du=0.2097, Dv=0.105) assumes a UNIT-weight Laplacian. Effective
  diffusion was running at 1/6 strength, so most presets froze as the
  reaction term couldn't compete with the weak spread. Removed /6.
- dt was 1.0, which is right at the stability edge for Du=0.2097
  (limit ~1/(6·Du) ≈ 0.79). Lowered to 0.7 for headroom.

Rendering:
- stepCount=96 through a 3-unit volume meant step size ≈ texel size.
  Under camera orbit, the fixed-stride march aligned to different texel
  boundaries per frame, producing visible ribs that appeared to "change
  with the camera". Bumped to 256 and added a per-pixel hash-based
  start offset (stochastic sampling), which converts what would be
  coherent aliasing into smooth noise that bloom easily absorbs.
- Widened the soft-iso smoothstep band from ±0.04 to ±0.08 so sub-texel
  surfaces fade in rather than popping.

All five presets should now evolve recognizably within a few seconds:
Spots stabilizes into blobs, Mazes into maze patterns, Mitosis visibly
divides, Coral fills space, Worms produces worm-like channels.

* XR UI panel: text labels, ray reticle, fix arrow direction

- Added text labels (PREV / NEXT / current slider parameter) via a
  canvas-rasterized label atlas uploaded to a GPUTexture. Labels are
  redrawn on mode change with dynamic font sizing so long labels like
  "GRAVITY" auto-fit.
- Added a glowing reticle at the XR ray / panel intersection whenever
  a pinch is active, giving Vision Pro users visual feedback about
  where their gaze-then-hand-corrected ray is pointing (since natural
  input only exposes a ray during a pinch).
- Fixed chevron direction: prev button now points left (<) and next
  points right (>).
- Widened button boxes to 0.36 world units so each fits a chevron
  plus a 2:1 aspect label rect with breathing room.
- Non-uniform label atlas sub-rects (2:1 for buttons, 4:1 for slider
  label) so text isn't stretched when sampled at the panel surface.
- Single-enforcer mode-cycling refactor: introduced selectMode() used
  by both DOM tab clicks and XR prev/next so state.mode, DOM classes,
  and simulation registry move together.
- XR slider drags now call syncUIFromState() + saveState() on release
  so DOM sliders show the updated value when the user exits VR.

* reaction sim: remove visible cube, scatter-noise seed

Three user-visible issues addressed:

1. Invisible boundary cube: patterns were piling up against the hard
   clamped-coord texture edge and you could see the cube outline.
   Added Dirichlet boundary in the compute shader — a smooth band at
   r > 0.80 of half-size pulls every cell toward (u=1, v=0), creating
   a "reservoir" zone that absorbs patterns before they reach the wall.
   Patterns now die in the interior; they literally cannot escape.

2. Visible cube edges in render: added a spherical alpha falloff
   (1 - smoothstep(0.78, 0.95, radial distance)) so the render has no
   cube edges at all — only a soft spherical fade to invisible.

3. "Keeps growing the same blobs again" on reset: the old seed was 12
   big spherical blobs in a fixed central region, so every reset
   produced the same general silhouette regardless of preset. Replaced
   with a scattered noise seed — 80 tiny random points (1-2 cell
   radius) in the center 40% of the volume. Each reset now looks
   genuinely different and early-time patterns are fine-grained
   instead of obviously spherical.

The reset plumbing itself was already correct: applyPreset calls
resetCurrentSim which destroys + recreates the sim, so the factory
re-runs Math.random for every preset click.

* fix reaction time knob, bg race, nbody classic reserved word

1. Reaction time slider: timeScale now drives substep count, not dt.
   Previously multiplying into dt blew past the explicit-Euler stability
   bound (~0.79 for Du=0.2097), producing NaN and a disappearing sim.

2. Reaction render ping-pong was off-by-one: renderBGs[pong] was reading
   the STALE texture. Swapped mapping so render sees the latest write.

3. Error scope handlers were deleting whatever sim currently lived at
   simulations[mode], which could be a fresh one the user just reset.
   Now captures the sim instance and only drops it if still the same.

4. nbody.classic.render.wgsl: renamed struct field 'active' -> 'enabled'.
   'active' is a WGSL reserved keyword, caused compile failure at 26:26.

* XR UI panel: add grab handle for repositioning

Adds a visionOS-style grab bar at the bottom of the panel that lets
users pinch-drag to reposition the panel anywhere in 3D space.

- New 'grab' element type in hit-testing and hover encoding
- Thin rounded pill at y=-0.40 below the slider, lights up with
  camera.accent on hover
- Drag tracks the world-space delta between initial and current
  ray/Z-plane intersection, offsetting XR_UI_PANEL_CENTER each frame
- Panel position persists across pinch releases within a session
- Grab state cleanly cleared on selectend alongside other UI state

* persistence: derive saved modes from DEFAULTS registry

saveState/loadState previously hardcoded each sim mode by name, causing
reaction and physics_classic to be silently omitted. Rather than adding
to the manual list, loop over Object.keys(DEFAULTS) — the canonical mode
registry — so any future mode added to DEFAULTS is automatically persisted
with no further edits.

modes exist; the persistence layer now derives from it instead of
maintaining a parallel list.

fix(fluid): use dye alpha for surface height

Render shader was computing height from length(d.rgb), which scales
with the dye color vector's magnitude. Single mode (~1.12), rainbow
(~1.73), and temperature dyes therefore produced visibly different
surface heights for the same injected density. Use d.a — the
mode-invariant splat amount written by fluid.forces.wgsl — so all
presets render at the same scale.

and PRs keep their existing slot semantics.

absolute asset paths; multiplexer publishes into per-ref subdirectories
on gh-pages and generates an index page. PRs get their own pr-N slot.

Fix XR stereo: use per-view camera buffer offsets instead of overwriting

Root cause: sim.render() called device.queue.writeBuffer(cameraBuffer, 0, ...)
for each eye. writeBuffer is a queue operation that executes before any submitted
command buffer — so the right eye's write overwrote the left eye's data, and
both render passes used the right eye's view matrix.

Fix (proper approach, matching the WebXR barebones sample):
- Expand all camera buffers to CAMERA_STRIDE * 2 (2 × 256 bytes)
- Create per-view bind groups for all simulations and the grid renderer,
  each binding the camera buffer at its eye's 256-byte-aligned offset
- render() and renderGrid() accept viewIndex (default 0) and write to
  viewIndex * CAMERA_STRIDE, so left and right eye data coexist in the
  buffer without conflict
- Single encoder + single submit preserved — no extra overhead
fix webxr render

Move XR viewer back 5m so simulation is in front, not surrounding

Apply getOffsetReferenceSpace() after acquiring the reference space to shift
the user's reported pose 5 units behind the world origin (z: -5 in WebGL
forward convention). Simulation geometry at [-2,2]^3 then appears 5m ahead.

For local-floor, also apply y: +1.6 to raise the reference space to eye level
so the simulation center is at roughly eye height rather than at the user's feet.

Use npm install instead of npm ci in deploy workflow

Vite 8 rolldown has platform-native bindings. Lockfile generated on macOS
arm64 doesn't include Linux x64 emnapi packages CI needs, so npm ci fails.
npm install resolves correctly per-platform without lockfile sync errors.

WebGPU compute shader playground with WebXR stereo rendering

Single-file HTML playground for experimenting with WebGPU compute shaders.
Four simulation modes (boids, N-body physics, fluid dynamics, parametric
shapes), each with interactive controls, presets, and real-time parameter
modulation. Features include:

- 3D rendering with orbit camera, background reference grid with animated pulses
- WebXR stereo rendering for Apple Vision Pro (XRGPUBinding path)
- Color palette system with 10 curated themes from popular editor themes
- Ctrl+drag interaction (attractor for boids/N-body, dye injection for fluid,
  surface deformation for parametric shapes)
- Shader debug panel with live editing and recompilation
- State persistence to localStorage with Reset All button
- HTTPS dev server for Vision Pro testing (self-signed cert)

Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider) (#1)

* add Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulation (3D)

New simulation mode: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion on a 3D volume,
rendered via fullscreen raymarch of an isosurface on the v-field.
Introduces the first texture-backed sim state (rg16float 3D ping-pong)
and the first raymarched renderer in the codebase — plumbing that
follow-up sims (Physarum, wave equation) can reuse.

Presets: Spots, Mazes, Worms, Mitosis, Coral.

* Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider)

Renders a floating 3D panel in immersive-vr mode so users can switch
simulation modes and tweak one primary parameter without leaving the
headset.

- New procedural SDF shader (src/shaders/xr.ui.wgsl) draws a rounded
  background, two chevron buttons, and a slider track/fill/knob in one
  quad — no textures, no glyph atlas.
- Per-mode slider bindings in main.ts map to an existing state field
  (e.g. fluid.forceStrength, physics.G).
- Ray-plane hit-testing routes pinch input between UI and simulation
  through a single-enforcer check in updateXRSimulationInteraction.
- selectstart defers resolution to the first XRFrame so hit-tests have
  a real pose; selectend triggers button actions or cleanly clears.
- UI renders into the HDR scene between sim.render and bloom so it
  picks up tonemap + glow.

* fix reaction sim black-screen and add per-frame error surfacing

Two bugs in the initial reaction-diffusion implementation:

1. rg16float is NOT in WebGPU's baseline storage-texture format list.
   Texture creation failed silently and the sim rendered garbage or
   nothing at all. Switched to rgba16float (always in the baseline),
   using only .rg channels in the shader. Fixed bytesPerRow (N*4 → N*8)
   and seed array stride (*2 → *4) accordingly.

2. Sim failures produced zero user feedback — black screen or freeze,
   with no way to know what went wrong. Now:

   - ensureSimulation() wraps factory creation in validation/internal/
     OOM error scopes. Any failure surfaces via showSimError() into the
     existing top-left red overlay.

   - frame() wraps sim.compute/render in a frame-level validation scope
     plus a try/catch. On any failure the broken sim is destroyed and
     dropped from the registry so the app stops hammering bad GPU state
     every frame. The user sees the error and can switch modes.

This addresses the "black screen / freeze / eject from XR" experience —
whatever goes wrong next will say so.

* reaction sim: fix diffusion scaling + raymarch aliasing

Two linked bugs produced the "frozen boxes with ribs" look and the
"changes when I move the camera" artifact:

Physics:
- The Laplacian divided neighbors by 6, but the canonical Gray-Scott
  atlas (Du=0.2097, Dv=0.105) assumes a UNIT-weight Laplacian. Effective
  diffusion was running at 1/6 strength, so most presets froze as the
  reaction term couldn't compete with the weak spread. Removed /6.
- dt was 1.0, which is right at the stability edge for Du=0.2097
  (limit ~1/(6·Du) ≈ 0.79). Lowered to 0.7 for headroom.

Rendering:
- stepCount=96 through a 3-unit volume meant step size ≈ texel size.
  Under camera orbit, the fixed-stride march aligned to different texel
  boundaries per frame, producing visible ribs that appeared to "change
  with the camera". Bumped to 256 and added a per-pixel hash-based
  start offset (stochastic sampling), which converts what would be
  coherent aliasing into smooth noise that bloom easily absorbs.
- Widened the soft-iso smoothstep band from ±0.04 to ±0.08 so sub-texel
  surfaces fade in rather than popping.

All five presets should now evolve recognizably within a few seconds:
Spots stabilizes into blobs, Mazes into maze patterns, Mitosis visibly
divides, Coral fills space, Worms produces worm-like channels.

* XR UI panel: text labels, ray reticle, fix arrow direction

- Added text labels (PREV / NEXT / current slider parameter) via a
  canvas-rasterized label atlas uploaded to a GPUTexture. Labels are
  redrawn on mode change with dynamic font sizing so long labels like
  "GRAVITY" auto-fit.
- Added a glowing reticle at the XR ray / panel intersection whenever
  a pinch is active, giving Vision Pro users visual feedback about
  where their gaze-then-hand-corrected ray is pointing (since natural
  input only exposes a ray during a pinch).
- Fixed chevron direction: prev button now points left (<) and next
  points right (>).
- Widened button boxes to 0.36 world units so each fits a chevron
  plus a 2:1 aspect label rect with breathing room.
- Non-uniform label atlas sub-rects (2:1 for buttons, 4:1 for slider
  label) so text isn't stretched when sampled at the panel surface.
- Single-enforcer mode-cycling refactor: introduced selectMode() used
  by both DOM tab clicks and XR prev/next so state.mode, DOM classes,
  and simulation registry move together.
- XR slider drags now call syncUIFromState() + saveState() on release
  so DOM sliders show the updated value when the user exits VR.

* reaction sim: remove visible cube, scatter-noise seed

Three user-visible issues addressed:

1. Invisible boundary cube: patterns were piling up against the hard
   clamped-coord texture edge and you could see the cube outline.
   Added Dirichlet boundary in the compute shader — a smooth band at
   r > 0.80 of half-size pulls every cell toward (u=1, v=0), creating
   a "reservoir" zone that absorbs patterns before they reach the wall.
   Patterns now die in the interior; they literally cannot escape.

2. Visible cube edges in render: added a spherical alpha falloff
   (1 - smoothstep(0.78, 0.95, radial distance)) so the render has no
   cube edges at all — only a soft spherical fade to invisible.

3. "Keeps growing the same blobs again" on reset: the old seed was 12
   big spherical blobs in a fixed central region, so every reset
   produced the same general silhouette regardless of preset. Replaced
   with a scattered noise seed — 80 tiny random points (1-2 cell
   radius) in the center 40% of the volume. Each reset now looks
   genuinely different and early-time patterns are fine-grained
   instead of obviously spherical.

The reset plumbing itself was already correct: applyPreset calls
resetCurrentSim which destroys + recreates the sim, so the factory
re-runs Math.random for every preset click.

* fix reaction time knob, bg race, nbody classic reserved word

1. Reaction time slider: timeScale now drives substep count, not dt.
   Previously multiplying into dt blew past the explicit-Euler stability
   bound (~0.79 for Du=0.2097), producing NaN and a disappearing sim.

2. Reaction render ping-pong was off-by-one: renderBGs[pong] was reading
   the STALE texture. Swapped mapping so render sees the latest write.

3. Error scope handlers were deleting whatever sim currently lived at
   simulations[mode], which could be a fresh one the user just reset.
   Now captures the sim instance and only drops it if still the same.

4. nbody.classic.render.wgsl: renamed struct field 'active' -> 'enabled'.
   'active' is a WGSL reserved keyword, caused compile failure at 26:26.

* XR UI panel: add grab handle for repositioning

Adds a visionOS-style grab bar at the bottom of the panel that lets
users pinch-drag to reposition the panel anywhere in 3D space.

- New 'grab' element type in hit-testing and hover encoding
- Thin rounded pill at y=-0.40 below the slider, lights up with
  camera.accent on hover
- Drag tracks the world-space delta between initial and current
  ray/Z-plane intersection, offsetting XR_UI_PANEL_CENTER each frame
- Panel position persists across pinch releases within a session
- Grab state cleanly cleared on selectend alongside other UI state

reaction and physics_classic to be silently omitted. Rather than adding
to the manual list, loop over Object.keys(DEFAULTS) — the canonical mode
registry — so any future mode added to DEFAULTS is automatically persisted
with no further edits.

maintaining a parallel list.

fix(fluid): use dye alpha for surface height

Render shader was computing height from length(d.rgb), which scales
with the dye color vector's magnitude. Single mode (~1.12), rainbow
(~1.73), and temperature dyes therefore produced visibly different
surface heights for the same injected density. Use d.a — the
mode-invariant splat amount written by fluid.forces.wgsl — so all
presets render at the same scale.

and PRs keep their existing slot semantics.

on gh-pages and generates an index page. PRs get their own pr-N slot.

Fix XR stereo: use per-view camera buffer offsets instead of overwriting

Root cause: sim.render() called device.queue.writeBuffer(cameraBuffer, 0, ...)
for each eye. writeBuffer is a queue operation that executes before any submitted
command buffer — so the right eye's write overwrote the left eye's data, and
both render passes used the right eye's view matrix.

Fix (proper approach, matching the WebXR barebones sample):
- Expand all camera buffers to CAMERA_STRIDE * 2 (2 × 256 bytes)
- Create per-view bind groups for all simulations and the grid renderer,
  each binding the camera buffer at its eye's 256-byte-aligned offset
- render() and renderGrid() accept viewIndex (default 0) and write to
  viewIndex * CAMERA_STRIDE, so left and right eye data coexist in the
  buffer without conflict
- Single encoder + single submit preserved — no extra overhead
fix webxr render

Move XR viewer back 5m so simulation is in front, not surrounding

Apply getOffsetReferenceSpace() after acquiring the reference space to shift
the user's reported pose 5 units behind the world origin (z: -5 in WebGL
forward convention). Simulation geometry at [-2,2]^3 then appears 5m ahead.

For local-floor, also apply y: +1.6 to raise the reference space to eye level
so the simulation center is at roughly eye height rather than at the user's feet.

Use npm install instead of npm ci in deploy workflow

Vite 8 rolldown has platform-native bindings. Lockfile generated on macOS
arm64 doesn't include Linux x64 emnapi packages CI needs, so npm ci fails.
npm install resolves correctly per-platform without lockfile sync errors.

WebGPU compute shader playground with WebXR stereo rendering

Single-file HTML playground for experimenting with WebGPU compute shaders.
Four simulation modes (boids, N-body physics, fluid dynamics, parametric
shapes), each with interactive controls, presets, and real-time parameter
modulation. Features include:

- 3D rendering with orbit camera, background reference grid with animated pulses
- WebXR stereo rendering for Apple Vision Pro (XRGPUBinding path)
- Color palette system with 10 curated themes from popular editor themes
- Ctrl+drag interaction (attractor for boids/N-body, dye injection for fluid,
  surface deformation for parametric shapes)

- State persistence to localStorage with Reset All button

  128: 37-39 fps
  256: 40-43 fps
All within noise. Bottleneck is force evaluation count (80K × 8192 = 655M/frame),
not workgroup size. Using 64 for lowest shared memory pressure.

fix: count-independent init velocity for spiral galaxy

Init circular velocity now uses fixed REF_SOURCES=1000 * avgMass as the
reference total mass, so vCirc doesn't scale with particle count.
Previously, enclosedMass scaled with MASSIVE_BODY_COUNT while G_eff
divided by sqrt(MASSIVE_BODY_COUNT), giving sqrt(N) speed dependence.

Results: virial at t=0 now 0.74 (10K) and 1.09 (100K) instead of
0.58 and 0.55. 100K reaches near-perfect equilibrium (virial=0.98)
with no controller adjustment needed (tidal stays at 0.005).

fix: calibrate PE estimate + bidirectional virial controller

Stats shader: use actual mass (not clamped), G_eff (not G_raw), avg mass 0.9.
Controller: bidirectional — increases tidal when sub-virial, increases damping when super-virial.
Results: virial 0.58→0.85, armContrast 0.33→1.32 over 46s, system stable.

feat: virial equilibrium controller + GPU stats reduction shader

New nbody.stats.wgsl: single-workgroup reduction computing KE, PE estimate,
rmsRadius, rmsHeight, angular momentum, total mass in one pass.

Bidirectional virial controller (runs once/second):
- virial > target → increase damping + decrease tidal (cool the system)
- virial < target → increase tidal + decrease damping (heat the system)

Dynamic damping and tidal strength auto-adjust to maintain equilibrium.
Exposed via window.__simStats() for diagnostics.

NOTE: PE estimate in stats shader needs calibration — virial ratios
currently read too high (7-47 instead of ~1), likely due to G normalization
being applied twice. Controller logic is correct but input is wrong.

of N-body noise in differentially rotating disk). Based on maxw179/barnes-hut.

Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider) (#1)

* add Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulation (3D)

New simulation mode: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion on a 3D volume,
rendered via fullscreen raymarch of an isosurface on the v-field.
Introduces the first texture-backed sim state (rg16float 3D ping-pong)
and the first raymarched renderer in the codebase — plumbing that
follow-up sims (Physarum, wave equation) can reuse.

Presets: Spots, Mazes, Worms, Mitosis, Coral.

* Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider)

Renders a floating 3D panel in immersive-vr mode so users can switch
simulation modes and tweak one primary parameter without leaving the
headset.

- New procedural SDF shader (src/shaders/xr.ui.wgsl) draws a rounded
  background, two chevron buttons, and a slider track/fill/knob in one
  quad — no textures, no glyph atlas.
- Per-mode slider bindings in main.ts map to an existing state field
  (e.g. fluid.forceStrength, physics.G).
- Ray-plane hit-testing routes pinch input between UI and simulation
  through a single-enforcer check in updateXRSimulationInteraction.
- selectstart defers resolution to the first XRFrame so hit-tests have
  a real pose; selectend triggers button actions or cleanly clears.
- UI renders into the HDR scene between sim.render and bloom so it
  picks up tonemap + glow.

* fix reaction sim black-screen and add per-frame error surfacing

Two bugs in the initial reaction-diffusion implementation:

1. rg16float is NOT in WebGPU's baseline storage-texture format list.
   Texture creation failed silently and the sim rendered garbage or
   nothing at all. Switched to rgba16float (always in the baseline),
   using only .rg channels in the shader. Fixed bytesPerRow (N*4 → N*8)
   and seed array stride (*2 → *4) accordingly.

2. Sim failures produced zero user feedback — black screen or freeze,
   with no way to know what went wrong. Now:

   - ensureSimulation() wraps factory creation in validation/internal/
     OOM error scopes. Any failure surfaces via showSimError() into the
     existing top-left red overlay.

   - frame() wraps sim.compute/render in a frame-level validation scope
     plus a try/catch. On any failure the broken sim is destroyed and
     dropped from the registry so the app stops hammering bad GPU state
     every frame. The user sees the error and can switch modes.

This addresses the "black screen / freeze / eject from XR" experience —
whatever goes wrong next will say so.

* reaction sim: fix diffusion scaling + raymarch aliasing

Two linked bugs produced the "frozen boxes with ribs" look and the
"changes when I move the camera" artifact:

Physics:
- The Laplacian divided neighbors by 6, but the canonical Gray-Scott
  atlas (Du=0.2097, Dv=0.105) assumes a UNIT-weight Laplacian. Effective
  diffusion was running at 1/6 strength, so most presets froze as the
  reaction term couldn't compete with the weak spread. Removed /6.
- dt was 1.0, which is right at the stability edge for Du=0.2097
  (limit ~1/(6·Du) ≈ 0.79). Lowered to 0.7 for headroom.

Rendering:
- stepCount=96 through a 3-unit volume meant step size ≈ texel size.
  Under camera orbit, the fixed-stride march aligned to different texel
  boundaries per frame, producing visible ribs that appeared to "change
  with the camera". Bumped to 256 and added a per-pixel hash-based
  start offset (stochastic sampling), which converts what would be
  coherent aliasing into smooth noise that bloom easily absorbs.
- Widened the soft-iso smoothstep band from ±0.04 to ±0.08 so sub-texel
  surfaces fade in rather than popping.

All five presets should now evolve recognizably within a few seconds:
Spots stabilizes into blobs, Mazes into maze patterns, Mitosis visibly
divides, Coral fills space, Worms produces worm-like channels.

* XR UI panel: text labels, ray reticle, fix arrow direction

- Added text labels (PREV / NEXT / current slider parameter) via a
  canvas-rasterized label atlas uploaded to a GPUTexture. Labels are
  redrawn on mode change with dynamic font sizing so long labels like
  "GRAVITY" auto-fit.
- Added a glowing reticle at the XR ray / panel intersection whenever
  a pinch is active, giving Vision Pro users visual feedback about
  where their gaze-then-hand-corrected ray is pointing (since natural
  input only exposes a ray during a pinch).
- Fixed chevron direction: prev button now points left (<) and next
  points right (>).
- Widened button boxes to 0.36 world units so each fits a chevron
  plus a 2:1 aspect label rect with breathing room.
- Non-uniform label atlas sub-rects (2:1 for buttons, 4:1 for slider
  label) so text isn't stretched when sampled at the panel surface.
- Single-enforcer mode-cycling refactor: introduced selectMode() used
  by both DOM tab clicks and XR prev/next so state.mode, DOM classes,
  and simulation registry move together.
- XR slider drags now call syncUIFromState() + saveState() on release
  so DOM sliders show the updated value when the user exits VR.

* reaction sim: remove visible cube, scatter-noise seed

Three user-visible issues addressed:

1. Invisible boundary cube: patterns were piling up against the hard
   clamped-coord texture edge and you could see the cube outline.
   Added Dirichlet boundary in the compute shader — a smooth band at
   r > 0.80 of half-size pulls every cell toward (u=1, v=0), creating
   a "reservoir" zone that absorbs patterns before they reach the wall.
   Patterns now die in the interior; they literally cannot escape.

2. Visible cube edges in render: added a spherical alpha falloff
   (1 - smoothstep(0.78, 0.95, radial distance)) so the render has no
   cube edges at all — only a soft spherical fade to invisible.

3. "Keeps growing the same blobs again" on reset: the old seed was 12
   big spherical blobs in a fixed central region, so every reset
   produced the same general silhouette regardless of preset. Replaced
   with a scattered noise seed — 80 tiny random points (1-2 cell
   radius) in the center 40% of the volume. Each reset now looks
   genuinely different and early-time patterns are fine-grained
   instead of obviously spherical.

The reset plumbing itself was already correct: applyPreset calls
resetCurrentSim which destroys + recreates the sim, so the factory
re-runs Math.random for every preset click.

* fix reaction time knob, bg race, nbody classic reserved word

1. Reaction time slider: timeScale now drives substep count, not dt.
   Previously multiplying into dt blew past the explicit-Euler stability
   bound (~0.79 for Du=0.2097), producing NaN and a disappearing sim.

2. Reaction render ping-pong was off-by-one: renderBGs[pong] was reading
   the STALE texture. Swapped mapping so render sees the latest write.

3. Error scope handlers were deleting whatever sim currently lived at
   simulations[mode], which could be a fresh one the user just reset.
   Now captures the sim instance and only drops it if still the same.

4. nbody.classic.render.wgsl: renamed struct field 'active' -> 'enabled'.
   'active' is a WGSL reserved keyword, caused compile failure at 26:26.

* XR UI panel: add grab handle for repositioning

Adds a visionOS-style grab bar at the bottom of the panel that lets
users pinch-drag to reposition the panel anywhere in 3D space.

- New 'grab' element type in hit-testing and hover encoding
- Thin rounded pill at y=-0.40 below the slider, lights up with
  camera.accent on hover
- Drag tracks the world-space delta between initial and current
  ray/Z-plane intersection, offsetting XR_UI_PANEL_CENTER each frame
- Panel position persists across pinch releases within a session
- Grab state cleanly cleared on selectend alongside other UI state

reaction and physics_classic to be silently omitted. Rather than adding
to the manual list, loop over Object.keys(DEFAULTS) — the canonical mode
registry — so any future mode added to DEFAULTS is automatically persisted
with no further edits.

fix(fluid): use dye alpha for surface height

Render shader was computing height from length(d.rgb), which scales
with the dye color vector's magnitude. Single mode (~1.12), rainbow
(~1.73), and temperature dyes therefore produced visibly different
surface heights for the same injected density. Use d.a — the
mode-invariant splat amount written by fluid.forces.wgsl — so all
presets render at the same scale.

and PRs keep their existing slot semantics.

on gh-pages and generates an index page. PRs get their own pr-N slot.

Fix XR stereo: use per-view camera buffer offsets instead of overwriting

Root cause: sim.render() called device.queue.writeBuffer(cameraBuffer, 0, ...)
for each eye. writeBuffer is a queue operation that executes before any submitted
command buffer — so the right eye's write overwrote the left eye's data, and
both render passes used the right eye's view matrix.

Fix (proper approach, matching the WebXR barebones sample):
- Expand all camera buffers to CAMERA_STRIDE * 2 (2 × 256 bytes)
- Create per-view bind groups for all simulations and the grid renderer,
  each binding the camera buffer at its eye's 256-byte-aligned offset
- render() and renderGrid() accept viewIndex (default 0) and write to
  viewIndex * CAMERA_STRIDE, so left and right eye data coexist in the
  buffer without conflict
- Single encoder + single submit preserved — no extra overhead
fix webxr render

Move XR viewer back 5m so simulation is in front, not surrounding

Apply getOffsetReferenceSpace() after acquiring the reference space to shift
the user's reported pose 5 units behind the world origin (z: -5 in WebGL
forward convention). Simulation geometry at [-2,2]^3 then appears 5m ahead.

For local-floor, also apply y: +1.6 to raise the reference space to eye level
so the simulation center is at roughly eye height rather than at the user's feet.

Use npm install instead of npm ci in deploy workflow

Vite 8 rolldown has platform-native bindings. Lockfile generated on macOS
arm64 doesn't include Linux x64 emnapi packages CI needs, so npm ci fails.
npm install resolves correctly per-platform without lockfile sync errors.

WebGPU compute shader playground with WebXR stereo rendering

Single-file HTML playground for experimenting with WebGPU compute shaders.
Four simulation modes (boids, N-body physics, fluid dynamics, parametric
shapes), each with interactive controls, presets, and real-time parameter
modulation. Features include:

- 3D rendering with orbit camera, background reference grid with animated pulses
- WebXR stereo rendering for Apple Vision Pro (XRGPUBinding path)

- Ctrl+drag interaction (attractor for boids/N-body, dye injection for fluid,
  surface deformation for parametric shapes)

perf: revert tile size to 64 (benchmarked 64/128/256 — no significant difference)

Tile size benchmark at 80K particles (headless Chrome, macOS):
  64: 37-42 fps
  128: 37-39 fps
  256: 40-43 fps
All within noise. Bottleneck is force evaluation count (80K × 8192 = 655M/frame),
not workgroup size. Using 64 for lowest shared memory pressure.

feat: add FPS tracking to diagnostic output

Each diagnostic snapshot now reports FPS from __simState().
Headless Chrome baseline: 28-54 FPS across presets (GPU-limited in software).
Stats pass costs ~10% (35→32 fps at 100K) — not the primary bottleneck.

fix: count-independent init velocity for spiral galaxy

Init circular velocity now uses fixed REF_SOURCES=1000 * avgMass as the
reference total mass, so vCirc doesn't scale with particle count.
Previously, enclosedMass scaled with MASSIVE_BODY_COUNT while G_eff
divided by sqrt(MASSIVE_BODY_COUNT), giving sqrt(N) speed dependence.

Results: virial at t=0 now 0.74 (10K) and 1.09 (100K) instead of
0.58 and 0.55. 100K reaches near-perfect equilibrium (virial=0.98)
with no controller adjustment needed (tidal stays at 0.005).

fix: calibrate PE estimate + bidirectional virial controller

Stats shader: use actual mass (not clamped), G_eff (not G_raw), avg mass 0.9.
Controller: bidirectional — increases tidal when sub-virial, increases damping when super-virial.
Results: virial 0.58→0.85, armContrast 0.33→1.32 over 46s, system stable.

feat: virial equilibrium controller + GPU stats reduction shader

New nbody.stats.wgsl: single-workgroup reduction computing KE, PE estimate,
rmsRadius, rmsHeight, angular momentum, total mass in one pass.

Bidirectional virial controller (runs once/second):
- virial > target → increase damping + decrease tidal (cool the system)
- virial < target → increase tidal + decrease damping (heat the system)

Dynamic damping and tidal strength auto-adjust to maintain equilibrium.
Exposed via window.__simStats() for diagnostics.

NOTE: PE estimate in stats shader needs calibration — virial ratios
currently read too high (7-47 instead of ~1), likely due to G normalization
being applied twice. Controller logic is correct but input is wrong.

Based on Duke wave dark matter paper: persistent spiral structure requires
a rotating non-axisymmetric potential (our tidal quadrupole) balanced by
energy dissipation (their inelastic collisions → our global damping).

Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider) (#1)

* add Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulation (3D)

New simulation mode: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion on a 3D volume,
rendered via fullscreen raymarch of an isosurface on the v-field.
Introduces the first texture-backed sim state (rg16float 3D ping-pong)
and the first raymarched renderer in the codebase — plumbing that
follow-up sims (Physarum, wave equation) can reuse.

Presets: Spots, Mazes, Worms, Mitosis, Coral.

* Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider)

Renders a floating 3D panel in immersive-vr mode so users can switch
simulation modes and tweak one primary parameter without leaving the
headset.

- New procedural SDF shader (src/shaders/xr.ui.wgsl) draws a rounded
  background, two chevron buttons, and a slider track/fill/knob in one
  quad — no textures, no glyph atlas.
- Per-mode slider bindings in main.ts map to an existing state field
  (e.g. fluid.forceStrength, physics.G).
- Ray-plane hit-testing routes pinch input between UI and simulation
  through a single-enforcer check in updateXRSimulationInteraction.
- selectstart defers resolution to the first XRFrame so hit-tests have
  a real pose; selectend triggers button actions or cleanly clears.
- UI renders into the HDR scene between sim.render and bloom so it
  picks up tonemap + glow.

* fix reaction sim black-screen and add per-frame error surfacing

Two bugs in the initial reaction-diffusion implementation:

1. rg16float is NOT in WebGPU's baseline storage-texture format list.
   Texture creation failed silently and the sim rendered garbage or
   nothing at all. Switched to rgba16float (always in the baseline),
   using only .rg channels in the shader. Fixed bytesPerRow (N*4 → N*8)
   and seed array stride (*2 → *4) accordingly.

2. Sim failures produced zero user feedback — black screen or freeze,
   with no way to know what went wrong. Now:

   - ensureSimulation() wraps factory creation in validation/internal/
     OOM error scopes. Any failure surfaces via showSimError() into the
     existing top-left red overlay.

   - frame() wraps sim.compute/render in a frame-level validation scope
     plus a try/catch. On any failure the broken sim is destroyed and
     dropped from the registry so the app stops hammering bad GPU state
     every frame. The user sees the error and can switch modes.

This addresses the "black screen / freeze / eject from XR" experience —
whatever goes wrong next will say so.

* reaction sim: fix diffusion scaling + raymarch aliasing

Two linked bugs produced the "frozen boxes with ribs" look and the
"changes when I move the camera" artifact:

Physics:
- The Laplacian divided neighbors by 6, but the canonical Gray-Scott
  atlas (Du=0.2097, Dv=0.105) assumes a UNIT-weight Laplacian. Effective
  diffusion was running at 1/6 strength, so most presets froze as the
  reaction term couldn't compete with the weak spread. Removed /6.
- dt was 1.0, which is right at the stability edge for Du=0.2097
  (limit ~1/(6·Du) ≈ 0.79). Lowered to 0.7 for headroom.

Rendering:
- stepCount=96 through a 3-unit volume meant step size ≈ texel size.
  Under camera orbit, the fixed-stride march aligned to different texel
  boundaries per frame, producing visible ribs that appeared to "change
  with the camera". Bumped to 256 and added a per-pixel hash-based
  start offset (stochastic sampling), which converts what would be
  coherent aliasing into smooth noise that bloom easily absorbs.
- Widened the soft-iso smoothstep band from ±0.04 to ±0.08 so sub-texel
  surfaces fade in rather than popping.

All five presets should now evolve recognizably within a few seconds:
Spots stabilizes into blobs, Mazes into maze patterns, Mitosis visibly
divides, Coral fills space, Worms produces worm-like channels.

* XR UI panel: text labels, ray reticle, fix arrow direction

- Added text labels (PREV / NEXT / current slider parameter) via a
  canvas-rasterized label atlas uploaded to a GPUTexture. Labels are
  redrawn on mode change with dynamic font sizing so long labels like
  "GRAVITY" auto-fit.
- Added a glowing reticle at the XR ray / panel intersection whenever
  a pinch is active, giving Vision Pro users visual feedback about
  where their gaze-then-hand-corrected ray is pointing (since natural
  input only exposes a ray during a pinch).
- Fixed chevron direction: prev button now points left (<) and next
  points right (>).
- Widened button boxes to 0.36 world units so each fits a chevron
  plus a 2:1 aspect label rect with breathing room.
- Non-uniform label atlas sub-rects (2:1 for buttons, 4:1 for slider
  label) so text isn't stretched when sampled at the panel surface.
- Single-enforcer mode-cycling refactor: introduced selectMode() used
  by both DOM tab clicks and XR prev/next so state.mode, DOM classes,
  and simulation registry move together.
- XR slider drags now call syncUIFromState() + saveState() on release
  so DOM sliders show the updated value when the user exits VR.

* reaction sim: remove visible cube, scatter-noise seed

Three user-visible issues addressed:

1. Invisible boundary cube: patterns were piling up against the hard
   clamped-coord texture edge and you could see the cube outline.
   Added Dirichlet boundary in the compute shader — a smooth band at
   r > 0.80 of half-size pulls every cell toward (u=1, v=0), creating
   a "reservoir" zone that absorbs patterns before they reach the wall.
   Patterns now die in the interior; they literally cannot escape.

2. Visible cube edges in render: added a spherical alpha falloff
   (1 - smoothstep(0.78, 0.95, radial distance)) so the render has no
   cube edges at all — only a soft spherical fade to invisible.

3. "Keeps growing the same blobs again" on reset: the old seed was 12
   big spherical blobs in a fixed central region, so every reset
   produced the same general silhouette regardless of preset. Replaced
   with a scattered noise seed — 80 tiny random points (1-2 cell
   radius) in the center 40% of the volume. Each reset now looks
   genuinely different and early-time patterns are fine-grained
   instead of obviously spherical.

The reset plumbing itself was already correct: applyPreset calls
resetCurrentSim which destroys + recreates the sim, so the factory
re-runs Math.random for every preset click.

* fix reaction time knob, bg race, nbody classic reserved word

1. Reaction time slider: timeScale now drives substep count, not dt.
   Previously multiplying into dt blew past the explicit-Euler stability
   bound (~0.79 for Du=0.2097), producing NaN and a disappearing sim.

2. Reaction render ping-pong was off-by-one: renderBGs[pong] was reading
   the STALE texture. Swapped mapping so render sees the latest write.

3. Error scope handlers were deleting whatever sim currently lived at
   simulations[mode], which could be a fresh one the user just reset.
   Now captures the sim instance and only drops it if still the same.

4. nbody.classic.render.wgsl: renamed struct field 'active' -> 'enabled'.
   'active' is a WGSL reserved keyword, caused compile failure at 26:26.

* XR UI panel: add grab handle for repositioning

Adds a visionOS-style grab bar at the bottom of the panel that lets
users pinch-drag to reposition the panel anywhere in 3D space.

- New 'grab' element type in hit-testing and hover encoding
- Thin rounded pill at y=-0.40 below the slider, lights up with

- Drag tracks the world-space delta between initial and current
  ray/Z-plane intersection, offsetting XR_UI_PANEL_CENTER each frame
- Panel position persists across pinch releases within a session
- Grab state cleanly cleared on selectend alongside other UI state

reaction and physics_classic to be silently omitted. Rather than adding
to the manual list, loop over Object.keys(DEFAULTS) — the canonical mode
registry — so any future mode added to DEFAULTS is automatically persisted
with no further edits.

fix(fluid): use dye alpha for surface height

Render shader was computing height from length(d.rgb), which scales
with the dye color vector's magnitude. Single mode (~1.12), rainbow
(~1.73), and temperature dyes therefore produced visibly different
surface heights for the same injected density. Use d.a — the
mode-invariant splat amount written by fluid.forces.wgsl — so all
presets render at the same scale.

and PRs keep their existing slot semantics.

on gh-pages and generates an index page. PRs get their own pr-N slot.

Fix XR stereo: use per-view camera buffer offsets instead of overwriting

Root cause: sim.render() called device.queue.writeBuffer(cameraBuffer, 0, ...)
for each eye. writeBuffer is a queue operation that executes before any submitted
command buffer — so the right eye's write overwrote the left eye's data, and
both render passes used the right eye's view matrix.

Fix (proper approach, matching the WebXR barebones sample):
- Expand all camera buffers to CAMERA_STRIDE * 2 (2 × 256 bytes)
- Create per-view bind groups for all simulations and the grid renderer,
  each binding the camera buffer at its eye's 256-byte-aligned offset
- render() and renderGrid() accept viewIndex (default 0) and write to
  viewIndex * CAMERA_STRIDE, so left and right eye data coexist in the
  buffer without conflict
- Single encoder + single submit preserved — no extra overhead
fix webxr render

Move XR viewer back 5m so simulation is in front, not surrounding

Apply getOffsetReferenceSpace() after acquiring the reference space to shift
the user's reported pose 5 units behind the world origin (z: -5 in WebGL
forward convention). Simulation geometry at [-2,2]^3 then appears 5m ahead.

For local-floor, also apply y: +1.6 to raise the reference space to eye level
so the simulation center is at roughly eye height rather than at the user's feet.

Use npm install instead of npm ci in deploy workflow

Vite 8 rolldown has platform-native bindings. Lockfile generated on macOS
arm64 doesn't include Linux x64 emnapi packages CI needs, so npm ci fails.
npm install resolves correctly per-platform without lockfile sync errors.

WebGPU compute shader playground with WebXR stereo rendering

Single-file HTML playground for experimenting with WebGPU compute shaders.
Four simulation modes (boids, N-body physics, fluid dynamics, parametric
shapes), each with interactive controls, presets, and real-time parameter
modulation. Features include:

- 3D rendering with orbit camera, background reference grid with animated pulses
- WebXR stereo rendering for Apple Vision Pro (XRGPUBinding path)
- Color palette system with 10 curated themes from popular editor themes
- Ctrl+drag interaction (attractor for boids/N-body, dye injection for fluid,
  surface deformation for parametric shapes)
- Shader debug panel with live editing and recompilation
- State persistence to localStorage with Reset All button
- HTTPS dev server for Vision Pro testing (self-signed cert)

feat: add FPS tracking to diagnostic output

Each diagnostic snapshot now reports FPS from __simState().
Headless Chrome baseline: 28-54 FPS across presets (GPU-limited in software).
Stats pass costs ~10% (35→32 fps at 100K) — not the primary bottleneck.

fix: count-independent init velocity for spiral galaxy

Init circular velocity now uses fixed REF_SOURCES=1000 * avgMass as the
reference total mass, so vCirc doesn't scale with particle count.
Previously, enclosedMass scaled with MASSIVE_BODY_COUNT while G_eff
divided by sqrt(MASSIVE_BODY_COUNT), giving sqrt(N) speed dependence.

Results: virial at t=0 now 0.74 (10K) and 1.09 (100K) instead of
0.58 and 0.55. 100K reaches near-perfect equilibrium (virial=0.98)
with no controller adjustment needed (tidal stays at 0.005).

fix: calibrate PE estimate + bidirectional virial controller

Stats shader: use actual mass (not clamped), G_eff (not G_raw), avg mass 0.9.
Controller: bidirectional — increases tidal when sub-virial, increases damping when super-virial.
Results: virial 0.58→0.85, armContrast 0.33→1.32 over 46s, system stable.

feat: virial equilibrium controller + GPU stats reduction shader

New nbody.stats.wgsl: single-workgroup reduction computing KE, PE estimate,
rmsRadius, rmsHeight, angular momentum, total mass in one pass.

Bidirectional virial controller (runs once/second):
- virial > target → increase damping + decrease tidal (cool the system)
- virial < target → increase tidal + decrease damping (heat the system)

Dynamic damping and tidal strength auto-adjust to maintain equilibrium.
Exposed via window.__simStats() for diagnostics.

NOTE: PE estimate in stats shader needs calibration — virial ratios
currently read too high (7-47 instead of ~1), likely due to G normalization
being applied twice. Controller logic is correct but input is wrong.

feat: spiral galaxy — rotating tidal bar + global damping equilibrium

Based on Duke wave dark matter paper: persistent spiral structure requires
a rotating non-axisymmetric potential (our tidal quadrupole) balanced by
energy dissipation (their inelastic collisions → our global damping).

tidal=0.03, damping=0.98: energy injection ≈ dissipation
- rmsRadius stable at 1.1-1.4 over 30s (radiusRate: -0.008)

Diagnostics over 30s:

feat: N-body simulation redesign — slower convergence, emergent structure, richer visuals

perf: tiled N-body compute with shared memory, eliminate hot-loop waste

Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider) (#1)

* add Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulation (3D)

New simulation mode: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion on a 3D volume,
rendered via fullscreen raymarch of an isosurface on the v-field.
Introduces the first texture-backed sim state (rg16float 3D ping-pong)
and the first raymarched renderer in the codebase — plumbing that
follow-up sims (Physarum, wave equation) can reuse.

Presets: Spots, Mazes, Worms, Mitosis, Coral.

* Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider)

Renders a floating 3D panel in immersive-vr mode so users can switch
simulation modes and tweak one primary parameter without leaving the
headset.

- New procedural SDF shader (src/shaders/xr.ui.wgsl) draws a rounded
  background, two chevron buttons, and a slider track/fill/knob in one
  quad — no textures, no glyph atlas.
- Per-mode slider bindings in main.ts map to an existing state field
  (e.g. fluid.forceStrength, physics.G).
- Ray-plane hit-testing routes pinch input between UI and simulation
  through a single-enforcer check in updateXRSimulationInteraction.
- selectstart defers resolution to the first XRFrame so hit-tests have
  a real pose; selectend triggers button actions or cleanly clears.
- UI renders into the HDR scene between sim.render and bloom so it
  picks up tonemap + glow.

* fix reaction sim black-screen and add per-frame error surfacing

Two bugs in the initial reaction-diffusion implementation:

1. rg16float is NOT in WebGPU's baseline storage-texture format list.
   Texture creation failed silently and the sim rendered garbage or
   nothing at all. Switched to rgba16float (always in the baseline),
   using only .rg channels in the shader. Fixed bytesPerRow (N*4 → N*8)
   and seed array stride (*2 → *4) accordingly.

2. Sim failures produced zero user feedback — black screen or freeze,
   with no way to know what went wrong. Now:

   - ensureSimulation() wraps factory creation in validation/internal/
     OOM error scopes. Any failure surfaces via showSimError() into the
     existing top-left red overlay.

   - frame() wraps sim.compute/render in a frame-level validation scope
     plus a try/catch. On any failure the broken sim is destroyed and
     dropped from the registry so the app stops hammering bad GPU state
     every frame. The user sees the error and can switch modes.

This addresses the "black screen / freeze / eject from XR" experience —
whatever goes wrong next will say so.

* reaction sim: fix diffusion scaling + raymarch aliasing

Two linked bugs produced the "frozen boxes with ribs" look and the
"changes when I move the camera" artifact:

Physics:
- The Laplacian divided neighbors by 6, but the canonical Gray-Scott
  atlas (Du=0.2097, Dv=0.105) assumes a UNIT-weight Laplacian. Effective
  diffusion was running at 1/6 strength, so most presets froze as the
  reaction term couldn't compete with the weak spread. Removed /6.
- dt was 1.0, which is right at the stability edge for Du=0.2097
  (limit ~1/(6·Du) ≈ 0.79). Lowered to 0.7 for headroom.

Rendering:
- stepCount=96 through a 3-unit volume meant step size ≈ texel size.
  Under camera orbit, the fixed-stride march aligned to different texel
  boundaries per frame, producing visible ribs that appeared to "change
  with the camera". Bumped to 256 and added a per-pixel hash-based
  start offset (stochastic sampling), which converts what would be
  coherent aliasing into smooth noise that bloom easily absorbs.
- Widened the soft-iso smoothstep band from ±0.04 to ±0.08 so sub-texel
  surfaces fade in rather than popping.

All five presets should now evolve recognizably within a few seconds:
Spots stabilizes into blobs, Mazes into maze patterns, Mitosis visibly
divides, Coral fills space, Worms produces worm-like channels.

* XR UI panel: text labels, ray reticle, fix arrow direction

- Added text labels (PREV / NEXT / current slider parameter) via a
  canvas-rasterized label atlas uploaded to a GPUTexture. Labels are
  redrawn on mode change with dynamic font sizing so long labels like
  "GRAVITY" auto-fit.
- Added a glowing reticle at the XR ray / panel intersection whenever
  a pinch is active, giving Vision Pro users visual feedback about
  where their gaze-then-hand-corrected ray is pointing (since natural
  input only exposes a ray during a pinch).
- Fixed chevron direction: prev button now points left (<) and next
  points right (>).
- Widened button boxes to 0.36 world units so each fits a chevron
  plus a 2:1 aspect label rect with breathing room.
- Non-uniform label atlas sub-rects (2:1 for buttons, 4:1 for slider
  label) so text isn't stretched when sampled at the panel surface.
- Single-enforcer mode-cycling refactor: introduced selectMode() used
  by both DOM tab clicks and XR prev/next so state.mode, DOM classes,
  and simulation registry move together.
- XR slider drags now call syncUIFromState() + saveState() on release
  so DOM sliders show the updated value when the user exits VR.

* reaction sim: remove visible cube, scatter-noise seed

Three user-visible issues addressed:

1. Invisible boundary cube: patterns were piling up against the hard
   clamped-coord texture edge and you could see the cube outline.
   Added Dirichlet boundary in the compute shader — a smooth band at
   r > 0.80 of half-size pulls every cell toward (u=1, v=0), creating
   a "reservoir" zone that absorbs patterns before they reach the wall.
   Patterns now die in the interior; they literally cannot escape.

2. Visible cube edges in render: added a spherical alpha falloff
   (1 - smoothstep(0.78, 0.95, radial distance)) so the render has no
   cube edges at all — only a soft spherical fade to invisible.

3. "Keeps growing the same blobs again" on reset: the old seed was 12
   big spherical blobs in a fixed central region, so every reset
   produced the same general silhouette regardless of preset. Replaced
   with a scattered noise seed — 80 tiny random points (1-2 cell
   radius) in the center 40% of the volume. Each reset now looks
   genuinely different and early-time patterns are fine-grained
   instead of obviously spherical.

The reset plumbing itself was already correct: applyPreset calls
resetCurrentSim which destroys + recreates the sim, so the factory
re-runs Math.random for every preset click.

* fix reaction time knob, bg race, nbody classic reserved word

1. Reaction time slider: timeScale now drives substep count, not dt.
   Previously multiplying into dt blew past the explicit-Euler stability
   bound (~0.79 for Du=0.2097), producing NaN and a disappearing sim.

2. Reaction render ping-pong was off-by-one: renderBGs[pong] was reading
   the STALE texture. Swapped mapping so render sees the latest write.

3. Error scope handlers were deleting whatever sim currently lived at
   simulations[mode], which could be a fresh one the user just reset.
   Now captures the sim instance and only drops it if still the same.

4. nbody.classic.render.wgsl: renamed struct field 'active' -> 'enabled'.
   'active' is a WGSL reserved keyword, caused compile failure at 26:26.

* XR UI panel: add grab handle for repositioning

Adds a visionOS-style grab bar at the bottom of the panel that lets
users pinch-drag to reposition the panel anywhere in 3D space.

- New 'grab' element type in hit-testing and hover encoding
- Thin rounded pill at y=-0.40 below the slider, lights up with

- Drag tracks the world-space delta between initial and current
  ray/Z-plane intersection, offsetting XR_UI_PANEL_CENTER each frame
- Panel position persists across pinch releases within a session
- Grab state cleanly cleared on selectend alongside other UI state

fix(fluid): use dye alpha for surface height

Render shader was computing height from length(d.rgb), which scales
with the dye color vector's magnitude. Single mode (~1.12), rainbow
(~1.73), and temperature dyes therefore produced visibly different
surface heights for the same injected density. Use d.a — the
mode-invariant splat amount written by fluid.forces.wgsl — so all
presets render at the same scale.

and PRs keep their existing slot semantics.

on gh-pages and generates an index page. PRs get their own pr-N slot.

Fix XR stereo: use per-view camera buffer offsets instead of overwriting

Root cause: sim.render() called device.queue.writeBuffer(cameraBuffer, 0, ...)
for each eye. writeBuffer is a queue operation that executes before any submitted
command buffer — so the right eye's write overwrote the left eye's data, and
both render passes used the right eye's view matrix.

Fix (proper approach, matching the WebXR barebones sample):
- Expand all camera buffers to CAMERA_STRIDE * 2 (2 × 256 bytes)
- Create per-view bind groups for all simulations and the grid renderer,
  each binding the camera buffer at its eye's 256-byte-aligned offset
- render() and renderGrid() accept viewIndex (default 0) and write to
  viewIndex * CAMERA_STRIDE, so left and right eye data coexist in the
  buffer without conflict
- Single encoder + single submit preserved — no extra overhead
fix webxr render

Move XR viewer back 5m so simulation is in front, not surrounding

Apply getOffsetReferenceSpace() after acquiring the reference space to shift
the user's reported pose 5 units behind the world origin (z: -5 in WebGL
forward convention). Simulation geometry at [-2,2]^3 then appears 5m ahead.

For local-floor, also apply y: +1.6 to raise the reference space to eye level
so the simulation center is at roughly eye height rather than at the user's feet.

Use npm install instead of npm ci in deploy workflow

Vite 8 rolldown has platform-native bindings. Lockfile generated on macOS
arm64 doesn't include Linux x64 emnapi packages CI needs, so npm ci fails.
npm install resolves correctly per-platform without lockfile sync errors.

WebGPU compute shader playground with WebXR stereo rendering

Single-file HTML playground for experimenting with WebGPU compute shaders.
Four simulation modes (boids, N-body physics, fluid dynamics, parametric
shapes), each with interactive controls, presets, and real-time parameter
modulation. Features include:

- 3D rendering with orbit camera, background reference grid with animated pulses
- WebXR stereo rendering for Apple Vision Pro (XRGPUBinding path)
- Color palette system with 10 curated themes from popular editor themes
- Ctrl+drag interaction (attractor for boids/N-body, dye injection for fluid,
  surface deformation for parametric shapes)
- Shader debug panel with live editing and recompilation
- State persistence to localStorage with Reset All button
- HTTPS dev server for Vision Pro testing (self-signed cert)

fix: count-independent init velocity for spiral galaxy

Init circular velocity now uses fixed REF_SOURCES=1000 * avgMass as the
reference total mass, so vCirc doesn't scale with particle count.
Previously, enclosedMass scaled with MASSIVE_BODY_COUNT while G_eff
divided by sqrt(MASSIVE_BODY_COUNT), giving sqrt(N) speed dependence.

Results: virial at t=0 now 0.74 (10K) and 1.09 (100K) instead of
0.58 and 0.55. 100K reaches near-perfect equilibrium (virial=0.98)
with no controller adjustment needed (tidal stays at 0.005).

fix: calibrate PE estimate + bidirectional virial controller

Stats shader: use actual mass (not clamped), G_eff (not G_raw), avg mass 0.9.
Controller: bidirectional — increases tidal when sub-virial, increases damping when super-virial.
Results: virial 0.58→0.85, armContrast 0.33→1.32 over 46s, system stable.

feat: virial equilibrium controller + GPU stats reduction shader

New nbody.stats.wgsl: single-workgroup reduction computing KE, PE estimate,
rmsRadius, rmsHeight, angular momentum, total mass in one pass.

Bidirectional virial controller (runs once/second):
- virial > target → increase damping + decrease tidal (cool the system)
- virial < target → increase tidal + decrease damping (heat the system)

Dynamic damping and tidal strength auto-adjust to maintain equilibrium.
Exposed via window.__simStats() for diagnostics.

NOTE: PE estimate in stats shader needs calibration — virial ratios
currently read too high (7-47 instead of ~1), likely due to G normalization
being applied twice. Controller logic is correct but input is wrong.

feat: spiral galaxy — rotating tidal bar + global damping equilibrium

Based on Duke wave dark matter paper: persistent spiral structure requires
a rotating non-axisymmetric potential (our tidal quadrupole) balanced by
energy dissipation (their inelastic collisions → our global damping).

tidal=0.03, damping=0.98: energy injection ≈ dissipation
- rmsRadius stable at 1.1-1.4 over 30s (radiusRate: -0.008)
- armContrast sustained: 0.83→1.26→0.82 (persists, doesn't dissolve)
- Flat disk: rmsH < 0.01 after t=7

Diagnostics over 30s:
- rmsRadius stable: 1.3→2.1 (slow expansion, contained)
- rmsHeight: 0.35→0.002 (flattens without going paper-thin due to init thickness)
- armContrast grows: 0.22→0.55→0.82→1.01 (arms strengthen over time!)

- Added COPY_SRC to particle buffers for readback support

feat: unique preset initial states + fix workgroup barrier uniform control flow

Presets now seed distinct particle geometries:
- Spiral Galaxy: logarithmic spiral arms with exponential radial profile
- Cosmic Web: shell with grid attractor perturbations for filaments
- Star Cluster: 5 merging Plummer-sphere sub-clumps
- Maelstrom: 4 counter-rotating tilted rings
- Dust Cloud: 150K particles with curl-noise turbulent velocity field
- Binary: two offset tilted sub-disks with tidal bridge

Fix workgroupBarrier uniform control flow: replace early return with
alive flag so all threads participate in barriers. Remove self-skip
branch from inner loop (softening handles it). Default count 80K.

feat: N-body simulation redesign — slower convergence, emergent structure, richer visuals

- Rebalanced force model: count-normalized gravity, reduced masses, wider spatial scale
- Rotating tidal quadrupole seeds spiral arms via differential rotation
- Count-scaled interaction with gravitational lensing composite effect
- 10-color stellar palette with per-particle PCG hash for visual variety
- Tracer subpopulations: retrograde, eccentric, halo orbits
- Disk recovery fades with distance — scattered particles drift freely
- Stripped pressure from inner loop, tile size 256, source cap 8192 for perf

perf: tiled N-body compute with shared memory, eliminate hot-loop waste

- Shared memory tiling: source bodies loaded cooperatively into
  var<workgroup> tile (128 bodies per tile as vec4f pos+mass).
  Reduces global memory reads ~64-100x per workgroup.
- inverseSqrt() replaces 1.0/sqrt() for native GPU rsqrt instruction.
- Eliminated redundant dot(diff,diff) in inner loop.
- Pre-allocate params staging buffer once at init instead of
  allocating ArrayBuffer + typed array views every frame.
- Workgroup size 64 -> 128 for better occupancy.

perf: remove per-frame GPU error scopes, clarify XR grab hit-test

- Remove pushErrorScope/popErrorScope from the render loop. These ran
  every frame, creating 60+ promise allocations/sec and microtask churn.
  device.onuncapturederror (already wired up) catches validation errors
  without per-frame overhead. Targeted scopes in ensureSimulation()
  remain for creation-time error attribution.

- Clarify XR UI panel comment: hit-test half-extents (e.g. GRAB_HALF_H
  = 0.035) are intentionally larger than the shader's rendered SDF
  extents (0.02) for easier targeting. The comment previously said
  "mirrored" which implied they should match.

Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider) (#1)

* add Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulation (3D)

New simulation mode: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion on a 3D volume,
rendered via fullscreen raymarch of an isosurface on the v-field.
Introduces the first texture-backed sim state (rg16float 3D ping-pong)
and the first raymarched renderer in the codebase — plumbing that
follow-up sims (Physarum, wave equation) can reuse.

Presets: Spots, Mazes, Worms, Mitosis, Coral.

* Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider)

Renders a floating 3D panel in immersive-vr mode so users can switch
simulation modes and tweak one primary parameter without leaving the
headset.

- New procedural SDF shader (src/shaders/xr.ui.wgsl) draws a rounded
  background, two chevron buttons, and a slider track/fill/knob in one
  quad — no textures, no glyph atlas.
- Per-mode slider bindings in main.ts map to an existing state field
  (e.g. fluid.forceStrength, physics.G).
- Ray-plane hit-testing routes pinch input between UI and simulation
  through a single-enforcer check in updateXRSimulationInteraction.
- selectstart defers resolution to the first XRFrame so hit-tests have
  a real pose; selectend triggers button actions or cleanly clears.
- UI renders into the HDR scene between sim.render and bloom so it
  picks up tonemap + glow.

* fix reaction sim black-screen and add per-frame error surfacing

Two bugs in the initial reaction-diffusion implementation:

1. rg16float is NOT in WebGPU's baseline storage-texture format list.
   Texture creation failed silently and the sim rendered garbage or
   nothing at all. Switched to rgba16float (always in the baseline),
   using only .rg channels in the shader. Fixed bytesPerRow (N*4 → N*8)
   and seed array stride (*2 → *4) accordingly.

2. Sim failures produced zero user feedback — black screen or freeze,
   with no way to know what went wrong. Now:

   - ensureSimulation() wraps factory creation in validation/internal/
     OOM error scopes. Any failure surfaces via showSimError() into the
     existing top-left red overlay.

   - frame() wraps sim.compute/render in a frame-level validation scope
     plus a try/catch. On any failure the broken sim is destroyed and
     dropped from the registry so the app stops hammering bad GPU state
     every frame. The user sees the error and can switch modes.

This addresses the "black screen / freeze / eject from XR" experience —
whatever goes wrong next will say so.

* reaction sim: fix diffusion scaling + raymarch aliasing

Two linked bugs produced the "frozen boxes with ribs" look and the
"changes when I move the camera" artifact:

Physics:
- The Laplacian divided neighbors by 6, but the canonical Gray-Scott
  atlas (Du=0.2097, Dv=0.105) assumes a UNIT-weight Laplacian. Effective
  diffusion was running at 1/6 strength, so most presets froze as the
  reaction term couldn't compete with the weak spread. Removed /6.
- dt was 1.0, which is right at the stability edge for Du=0.2097
  (limit ~1/(6·Du) ≈ 0.79). Lowered to 0.7 for headroom.

Rendering:
- stepCount=96 through a 3-unit volume meant step size ≈ texel size.
  Under camera orbit, the fixed-stride march aligned to different texel
  boundaries per frame, producing visible ribs that appeared to "change
  with the camera". Bumped to 256 and added a per-pixel hash-based
  start offset (stochastic sampling), which converts what would be
  coherent aliasing into smooth noise that bloom easily absorbs.
- Widened the soft-iso smoothstep band from ±0.04 to ±0.08 so sub-texel
  surfaces fade in rather than popping.

All five presets should now evolve recognizably within a few seconds:
Spots stabilizes into blobs, Mazes into maze patterns, Mitosis visibly
divides, Coral fills space, Worms produces worm-like channels.

* XR UI panel: text labels, ray reticle, fix arrow direction

- Added text labels (PREV / NEXT / current slider parameter) via a
  canvas-rasterized label atlas uploaded to a GPUTexture. Labels are
  redrawn on mode change with dynamic font sizing so long labels like
  "GRAVITY" auto-fit.
- Added a glowing reticle at the XR ray / panel intersection whenever
  a pinch is active, giving Vision Pro users visual feedback about
  where their gaze-then-hand-corrected ray is pointing (since natural
  input only exposes a ray during a pinch).
- Fixed chevron direction: prev button now points left (<) and next
  points right (>).
- Widened button boxes to 0.36 world units so each fits a chevron
  plus a 2:1 aspect label rect with breathing room.
- Non-uniform label atlas sub-rects (2:1 for buttons, 4:1 for slider
  label) so text isn't stretched when sampled at the panel surface.
- Single-enforcer mode-cycling refactor: introduced selectMode() used
  by both DOM tab clicks and XR prev/next so state.mode, DOM classes,
  and simulation registry move together.
- XR slider drags now call syncUIFromState() + saveState() on release
  so DOM sliders show the updated value when the user exits VR.

* reaction sim: remove visible cube, scatter-noise seed

Three user-visible issues addressed:

1. Invisible boundary cube: patterns were piling up against the hard
   clamped-coord texture edge and you could see the cube outline.
   Added Dirichlet boundary in the compute shader — a smooth band at
   r > 0.80 of half-size pulls every cell toward (u=1, v=0), creating
   a "reservoir" zone that absorbs patterns before they reach the wall.
   Patterns now die in the interior; they literally cannot escape.

2. Visible cube edges in render: added a spherical alpha falloff
   (1 - smoothstep(0.78, 0.95, radial distance)) so the render has no
   cube edges at all — only a soft spherical fade to invisible.

3. "Keeps growing the same blobs again" on reset: the old seed was 12
   big spherical blobs in a fixed central region, so every reset
   produced the same general silhouette regardless of preset. Replaced
   with a scattered noise seed — 80 tiny random points (1-2 cell
   radius) in the center 40% of the volume. Each reset now looks
   genuinely different and early-time patterns are fine-grained
   instead of obviously spherical.

The reset plumbing itself was already correct: applyPreset calls
resetCurrentSim which destroys + recreates the sim, so the factory
re-runs Math.random for every preset click.

* fix reaction time knob, bg race, nbody classic reserved word

1. Reaction time slider: timeScale now drives substep count, not dt.
   Previously multiplying into dt blew past the explicit-Euler stability
   bound (~0.79 for Du=0.2097), producing NaN and a disappearing sim.

2. Reaction render ping-pong was off-by-one: renderBGs[pong] was reading
   the STALE texture. Swapped mapping so render sees the latest write.

3. Error scope handlers were deleting whatever sim currently lived at
   simulations[mode], which could be a fresh one the user just reset.
   Now captures the sim instance and only drops it if still the same.

4. nbody.classic.render.wgsl: renamed struct field 'active' -> 'enabled'.
   'active' is a WGSL reserved keyword, caused compile failure at 26:26.

* XR UI panel: add grab handle for repositioning

Adds a visionOS-style grab bar at the bottom of the panel that lets
users pinch-drag to reposition the panel anywhere in 3D space.

- New 'grab' element type in hit-testing and hover encoding
- Thin rounded pill at y=-0.40 below the slider, lights up with

- Drag tracks the world-space delta between initial and current
  ray/Z-plane intersection, offsetting XR_UI_PANEL_CENTER each frame
- Panel position persists across pinch releases within a session
- Grab state cleanly cleared on selectend alongside other UI state

fix(fluid): use dye alpha for surface height

Render shader was computing height from length(d.rgb), which scales
with the dye color vector's magnitude. Single mode (~1.12), rainbow
(~1.73), and temperature dyes therefore produced visibly different
surface heights for the same injected density. Use d.a — the
mode-invariant splat amount written by fluid.forces.wgsl — so all
presets render at the same scale.

Fix XR stereo: use per-view camera buffer offsets instead of overwriting

Root cause: sim.render() called device.queue.writeBuffer(cameraBuffer, 0, ...)
for each eye. writeBuffer is a queue operation that executes before any submitted
command buffer — so the right eye's write overwrote the left eye's data, and
both render passes used the right eye's view matrix.

Fix (proper approach, matching the WebXR barebones sample):
- Expand all camera buffers to CAMERA_STRIDE * 2 (2 × 256 bytes)
- Create per-view bind groups for all simulations and the grid renderer,
  each binding the camera buffer at its eye's 256-byte-aligned offset
- render() and renderGrid() accept viewIndex (default 0) and write to
  viewIndex * CAMERA_STRIDE, so left and right eye data coexist in the
  buffer without conflict
- Single encoder + single submit preserved — no extra overhead
fix webxr render

Move XR viewer back 5m so simulation is in front, not surrounding

Apply getOffsetReferenceSpace() after acquiring the reference space to shift
the user's reported pose 5 units behind the world origin (z: -5 in WebGL
forward convention). Simulation geometry at [-2,2]^3 then appears 5m ahead.

For local-floor, also apply y: +1.6 to raise the reference space to eye level
so the simulation center is at roughly eye height rather than at the user's feet.

Use npm install instead of npm ci in deploy workflow

Vite 8 rolldown has platform-native bindings. Lockfile generated on macOS
arm64 doesn't include Linux x64 emnapi packages CI needs, so npm ci fails.
npm install resolves correctly per-platform without lockfile sync errors.

WebGPU compute shader playground with WebXR stereo rendering

Single-file HTML playground for experimenting with WebGPU compute shaders.
Four simulation modes (boids, N-body physics, fluid dynamics, parametric
shapes), each with interactive controls, presets, and real-time parameter
modulation. Features include:

- 3D rendering with orbit camera, background reference grid with animated pulses
- WebXR stereo rendering for Apple Vision Pro (XRGPUBinding path)
- Color palette system with 10 curated themes from popular editor themes
- Ctrl+drag interaction (attractor for boids/N-body, dye injection for fluid,
  surface deformation for parametric shapes)
- Shader debug panel with live editing and recompilation
- State persistence to localStorage with Reset All button
